classification.summary: Merchant experiences recurring operational failures with ShipBob including missed pickups/dropoffs, lost inventory, and slow warehouse responsiveness that compound significantly as their business scales beyond small parcels to containers and pallets.

content.body: I am looking forward to never having to work with this disaster of a business again. We have issues with them on a weekly basis and their support is almost nonexistent. We have been assigned new account managers several times over the past few months. Their billing is almost always incorrect, they love to charge for made-up expenses and then not be able to provide sufficient justification for them. It seems that they have just grown way too quickly without proper infrastructure. I would not recommend working with them. They are many other reliable fulfillment centers in the US where you have real customer support and boots on the ground.

classification.summary: Merchant is actively leaving ShipMonk due to chronic operational issues, poor support, billing inaccuracies, and overgrowth without adequate infrastructure.

content.body: The app works well except for printing problems from time to time.  However I would not recommend using their insurance - 'shipinsurance'.  The app allows you to buy insurance on an item even if it is not insurable.  I have been using the app assuming if I could buy insurance the item was insurable.  I have filed my first claim in two years and it has been denied as the shipping option was not insurable despite the fact that they offered insurance on my shipping option.  I would have chosen a different shipping option had I known this.  App needs to be fixed so that insurance is not offered on non-insurable shipping options.

classification.summary: Merchant frustrated with ShipStation's insurance feature offering coverage on non-insurable shipping options, resulting in a denied claim and requesting a product fix to prevent future issues.

content.body: Our work together with ShipBob Ireland has been amazing so far. The app is really intuitive and easy to use. They are always there to support us with all kinds of problems that occur and resolve them very quickly. The integration with Shopify is excellent. We would definitely recommend their service.

classification.summary: UK-based merchant praising ShipBob Ireland's intuitive app, responsive customer support, and seamless Shopify integration.

content.body: We have been using Shipbob in our US operations for over a year now and we recently made the decision to move the rest of our global fulfilment operations over to them too as we were so impressed with the service. Shipbob was very easy to install and has been a speedy and reliable way for us to process orders for our customers across the globe. The tracking and reporting on the order process gives me everything I need to check on each individual customer order and I am looking forward to making use of the custom features like processing delay and gift notes in future. The customer service so far has been very prompt and helpful - the team are very kind, friendly and always happy to help. I thoroughly recommend moving over to Shipbob for your fulfilment needs.

classification.summary: UK-based merchant is praising ShipBob's ease of use, reliability, speed, tracking capabilities, and customer service after successfully expanding global operations to the platform.

content.body: ShipBob has been the best 3PL we have used at GRJ Gloves.  We value their partnership and customer service very much.

classification.summary: GRJ Gloves highly values ShipBob's partnership and customer service, positioning them as their best 3PL provider to date.

content.body: We have been using ShipBob now for over 12 months and the most impressive thing has been how well it integrates with Shopify and Gorgias making both the processing of orders and handling of customer support inquiries and returns extremely easy saving us considerable amounts of time. Recently we also started selling on Amazon and the process of integrating with Amazon FBA and replenishment of inventory is also very simple and automated. If we ever had any questions or concerns about our implementation or process Ankit has been very efficient and responsive in helping us with any of our questions.

classification.summary: Australian merchant highly satisfied with ShipBob's Shopify and Amazon integration, ease of use, and responsive customer support over 12+ months of use.

content.body: This app is working well for me. It sends order information to Shipbob for products with Shipbob set as inventory source and sends the tracking information back. I also use it to sync inventory so that we don't oversell products. Thanks!

classification.summary: King Kong Apparel is satisfied with ShipBob's integration capabilities, particularly the seamless order syncing, tracking information retrieval, and inventory management features that prevent overselling.

content.body: Great communication & problem solving. The Shipbob team is always just an email away ready to help & assist!

classification.summary: Merchant praising ShipBob's responsive customer communication and problem-solving support team.

content.body:
I recently started using Shipbob for my e-commerce business and I have been blown away by the ease of installation, seamless integration with Shopify, and excellent customer service. The team, specifically Ankit Gupta and Mike Rottar, made the whole process extremely easy and enjoyable.
But what really sets Shipbob apart is the speed and reliability of their order ingestion. I have noticed a significant improvement in my fulfillment times and I have complete confidence that my orders will be processed accurately and efficiently.
Overall, I am extremely satisfied with Shipbob and highly recommend them to anyone looking to improve their fulfillment process. Thank you Ankit Gupta and Mike Rottar for making everything so awesome!
- PeterAffirmation Matchdeck

classification.summary: Affirmation Matchdeck is highly satisfied with ShipBob's seamless Shopify integration, fast order processing, reliable fulfillment, and exceptional customer service.

content.body: From start to finish, I have been supported and was guided through the entire process with Shipbob and Shopify integration by Ankit Gupta. All my questions were answered without hesitation and with honesty.

classification.summary: Merchant praises ShipBob's onboarding support and integration process, highlighting the personalized guidance and responsiveness provided by their account manager.

content.body: ShipBob is lightyears ahead in terms of accuracy, reliability, and responsiveness compared to our previous 3PL. We used to have a fulfillment center that made constant errors on shipments, rarely responded to our queries, and lost many of our packages. ShipBob is almost the complete opposite. Their team is very responsive to requests and transparent with upfront pricing, and overall very accurate with shipments. Would highly recommend.

classification.summary: RIFRUF is praising ShipBob's superior accuracy, reliability, and customer service compared to their previous 3PL provider, with no indication of switching or unresolved pain points.

content.body:
Software has powerful analyticsAlmost 100% order fulfillment rateRitesh has been a helpful account manager
Areas for improvement are investigating patterns in unsuccessfully delivered orders and improving processes to prevent the same error from happening for future orders.

classification.summary: Lumina praises ShipBob's analytics capabilities and high fulfillment rate while suggesting improvements to delivery error investigation and prevention processes.

content.body: Shipbob has been smoothly assisting our business in getting orders shipped out and managed via the connection to Shopify. Very responsive and Lacey has been helpful.

classification.summary: Ashley N. Kirkwood is satisfied with ShipBob's order fulfillment and Shopify integration, praising their responsiveness and support staff.

content.body:
The app and connection is excellent. Cant say enough how much it helps to have shipbob deeply integrated into our shopify backend. It makes linking items and tracking inventory smooth.
Appreciate all that the shipbob team and app has enabled for our store.

classification.summary: Wicked Cushions praises ShipBob's seamless Shopify integration and inventory management capabilities, expressing strong satisfaction with the platform and team.

content.body: We have recently moved to ShipBob and the transition has been incredibly smooth! This was definitely down to Lacey who guided us through every step and answered all of our countless queries. First impressions are that ShipBob is a well established company with great communication. So far, so good.

classification.summary: New ShipBob customer praising the smooth onboarding experience and excellent support from their onboarding specialist, with no complaints or pain points expressed.

content.body: ShipBob customer support is always very helpful and very quick to respond. Would recommend them for your needs. The ShipBob platform is easy to use and navigate.

classification.summary: HopSkipShop is satisfied with ShipBob's customer support responsiveness, platform usability, and recommends the service to others.

content.body: Very pleased with shipbob so far! My company has been using shipbob for over a year now and we are excited to have them apart of our growth as we look to expand our company! Their site is very user friendly and takes the leg work out of fulfilling orders so you can put your focus in other aspects of your company! #teamshipbob

classification.summary: Established ShipBob customer expressing satisfaction with the platform's user-friendly interface and how it enables business focus on growth and expansion.

content.body:
Shipbob has been able to offer great solutions to our needs from a 3PL. We were nervous about the change but now we don't look back!
Installation of the App was extremely easy and we had someone help us onboard who was there whenever we needed, so it made the process far from intimidating. When it comes to speed and reliability of order ingestion, the process is fairly straight forward and easy. If you have inventory ready, the process is seamless. I would say the only hiccup is with Amazon FBA orders but Shipbob is always willing to help. Overall when it comes to tracking upload speed, communication of order progress, and speed ShipBob has been a great partner. As with any 3PL there will always be some issues, it's the nature of the business, but it's good to know that we can rely on ShipBob's technical support team to get things figured out.

classification.summary: Komuso Design is satisfied with ShipBob's 3PL services, highlighting excellent onboarding support, fast order processing, and reliable customer service, with only minor challenges around Amazon FBA integration.

content.body: I've had a great experience with ShipBob so far!It was really easy to install, quick and reliable order ingestion. Simple and effective tracking and I'm especially impressed with how the inventory syncs with my Shopify store and how easy it is to order new deliveries to my chosen warehouse.The support I received from my dedicated Implementation Manager, Sergio was outstanding as he made everything so simple and responded immediately to all of my queries. Thank you Sergio!I'm looking forward to growing my business internationally and I feel confident that ShipBob is the best fulfilment partner to help me achieve my business goals.

classification.summary: UK-based merchant praising ShipBob's ease of use, reliable order ingestion, inventory syncing, and excellent support from their implementation manager, expressing confidence in the platform for international growth.

content.body: Shipbob has been great to work with so far. I love that I have a dedicated account rep who can help me sort out whatever issue may be happening. I definitely am very happy with the service.

classification.summary: Merchant is satisfied with ShipBob's service and appreciates having a dedicated account representative to resolve issues.

content.body: There are definitely pros and cons to shipbob. The shopify app works fine and they do have a lot of features, adding new tech features like EDI capability and integrations with SPSCommerce. However, on the b2b side, we have experienced issues. D2C works pretty seamlessly and connects to shopify with few issues.

classification.summary: Fera Pet Organics finds ShipBob's D2C and Shopify integration functional with good features, but experiences issues on the B2B side despite recent tech additions like EDI capability.

content.body:
Couldn't be happier with ShipBob. Things they offer critical to our success are:
Ease of installationSpeed and reliability of order ingestionValue-add features like inventory sync, rules, and order processing delayTechnical support

classification.summary: Merchant is highly satisfied with ShipBob's ease of use, speed, reliability, and value-added features including inventory sync and technical support.

content.body: We've got shipbob set up in 3 locations in the US, 1 in Canada, 1 in the EU, and 1 in the UK. It certainly reduces the amount of effort of managing multiple warehouse providers!

classification.summary: Antlion Audio praises ShipBob for simplifying multi-location warehouse management across 7 global fulfillment centers.

content.body:
Easy to use and integrates very well with Shopify.Very happy with it.
ShipBob has very responsive and helpful support for all our needs.

classification.summary: Canadian merchant is very satisfied with ShipBob's Shopify integration and responsive support.

content.body: Shipbob has made my fulfillment so much easier and efficient. My account manager, Neel B is incredible. He is always available, makes time to meet with me, and proactively shares methods for me to use to be more effective, efficient, and serve customers at a lower cost. I honestly view Neel as a member of my team and would feel lost without him. I appreciate this the most about Shipbob!

classification.summary: Kaizen Food Company is highly satisfied with ShipBob's fulfillment efficiency and particularly values their account manager Neel B's proactive support and strategic guidance.

content.body: Overall ShipBob has been a good experience. My merchant success specialist has been a really big help. I do wish the app/website was more mobile friendly. It's very hard to navigate and many of the features are next to impossible to do when not on a computer. I work 90% from my phone so it's a struggle. The shopify integration is pretty solid. I do notice quite a bit of delay sometimes with tracking crossing over from shipbob to shopify. Sometimes it never crosses over at all so I have to add it manually but it's pretty rare. No problems with order syncing, it's fast and accurate. Overall I would recommend!

classification.summary: Merchant is generally satisfied with ShipBob's service and specialist support but frustrated by poor mobile app/website usability and occasional tracking sync delays with Shopify.

content.body: Soo glad we went with ShipBob. Their software was super easy to integrate with our Shopify store and our implementation specialist Lacey became an indispensable part of our team while onboarding, helping us through every question we could throw her way. The ability to have rule automations in place, inventory sync, and re-order points are excellent features to have and we are able to replenish Amazon, process and ship DTC orders, and fulfill case level wholesale skus. 5 Stars!

classification.summary: Wunderground is highly satisfied with ShipBob's ease of integration, implementation support, and multi-channel fulfillment capabilities including DTC, Amazon, and wholesale operations.

content.body: We launched our first-ever e-commerce platform with Shopify and worked with ShipBob to manage all of our fulfillment.  Huge thanks to Lacey Elliott at ShipBob for making this process smooth and helping us whenever we needed it.   We would highly recommend ShipBob!- Ducks of a Feather marketing team

classification.summary: New ecommerce merchant praising ShipBob's fulfillment service and account manager support for smooth launch experience.

content.body: Shipbob has been great to work with. Easy integration and thorough training and set up. They have helped us worth though any issues we had and have extremely responsive support.

classification.summary: TakeShots is satisfied with ShipBob's easy integration, comprehensive training, responsive support, and effective issue resolution.

content.body: I am still new to the new ShipBob fulfillment experience, I am told things will pick up once I am more acclimated. So we are optimistic that is true

classification.summary: New ShipBob customer is still in early stages of onboarding and is optimistic that service quality and fulfillment performance will improve with familiarity.

content.body: The shipbob app is easy to use and the shopify integration is seamless. The realtime order sycing works well.

classification.summary: Wine Folly is satisfied with ShipBob's ease of use, seamless Shopify integration, and reliable real-time order syncing functionality.

content.body: Scaling your business with a solid fulfillment partner can be a much easier task. ShipBob has reliable customer support and a willingness to solve issues and complex matters; it can be a bumpy ride initially, but the earlier you start the cooperation, the easier it will be when you scale. The easiness of managing shipments beyond your domestic market is also a big plus, and ShipBob covers most of the e-commerce needs. The integration with various services is also a plus. Overall, highly recommended.

classification.summary: Calidad Tools praises ShipBob for reliable customer support, scalability, international shipping capabilities, and service integrations, recommending it highly despite an initial learning curve.

content.body:
Using the this app has made it very easy for us to manage our fulfilment across a number of countries and not just in North Americas so it has saved a lot of time.
It was very easy to install as wasn't a private app and already in app store and the pulling down of orders can be set around the time frames we want which has been very import for when customer realise they have used old address or just typed it incorrectly.
The tracking is also passed back into Shopify from Shipbob as soon as delivery in transit which is sent on to our customer so saves us time again and our customers know what is happening with their delivery.
Technical support is also very quick to update us on courier issues as well as setting up import rules and tags when we see fraudulent orders...again this has saved us time and money on orders going bad.
Highly recommend the app and the company. :-)

classification.summary: Australian merchant praising ShipBob for simplifying multi-country fulfillment management, order processing automation, tracking integration, and fraud detection support.

content.body: Great service, the support is responsive and the shipping time is great. Tracking works well and their platform is easy to use.

classification.summary: Positive review of ShipBob: Great service, the support is responsive and the shipping time is great. Tracking works well and their platform is easy

content.body: Shipbob is solid, it handles all of our fulfillment and it works as advertised. Easy to install, good functionality.

classification.summary: Lasso is satisfied with ShipBob's fulfillment services, finding the platform easy to use and reliable for their ecommerce needs.

content.body: We are very happy with ShipBob FulFillment app and highly recommend ShipBob as a warehouse partner! The Fulfillment app is easy to install, very fast and reliable order ingestion and has many value-add features like inventory-sync, order processing delay, etc. Technical support is also a 10/10.

classification.summary: Otherworld is very satisfied with ShipBob's fulfillment app, praising its ease of use, reliability, feature set, and technical support.

content.body: ShipBob is thorough with communication and great at assisting you in the setup process. It is easy to get set up and begin shipping! The merchant success specialists I have worked with have been great at answering questions and finding resolutions. There are some aspects we are not fond of, the costs of WROs and the precision required when sending supplies (we order from supply stores and have it sent directly there, so affixing labels is not always possible); they do not seem to work well with USPS (we have had 1 or 2 days in the last year where all of our orders from a specific day were labeled but never moved or it took a week or more for them to). All in all positive experience, I do recommend inquiring about fulfillment times if you are guaranteeing same-day shipping, etc.

classification.summary: The Fresh Test is satisfied with ShipBob's communication and setup support but has concerns about WRO costs, supply labeling requirements, and occasional USPS integration issues, though they remain a recommender overall.

content.body: user friendly app, easy to install and the team walk you through the whole process during an onboarding call.Integrates seamlessly with our shopify store and makes fulfilling orders to customers a much more automated process.

classification.summary: UK-based merchant praising ShipBob's user-friendly app, seamless Shopify integration, and effective onboarding process that streamlines order fulfillment.

content.body:
App works fine, but the customer service and pricing is TERRIBLE.
We would CONSTANTLY be charged many times more because Shipbob insisted on sending our products in boxes that were much larger than our products. We're talking about nearly a $100 difference because they didn't want to use a smaller box to ship (which they have).
We are a small business, so seeing a shipping cost of $140 for something that should cost $30-$40 is terrible. Moreover, having to go back and forth with customer service to fix this issue causes delays and makes our customers extremely unhappy. This is not the first time and after customer service was contacted, they would apologize, issue a partial refund, and repeat the mistake. If any higher-ups see this, I will be happy to provide proof of correspondence so things can be changed.
Be very, very careful if you are thinking of using Shipbob services.
UPDATE:
I was contacted by Shipbob's VP or Partnerships and Director of Onboarding services soon after posting this review. They were sincere with their apology about my experience so far and it showed that they took this matter seriously. It doesn't make up for everything that transpired, but I will add two stars for trying to make things right and bringing immediate attention to my case.

classification.summary: V.I.T.L. Backpacks experienced severe overcharging due to ShipBob using oversized boxes (charging $140 instead of $30-40) and poor customer service response, though ShipBob leadership's intervention prevented immediate switching.

content.body:

Overall, ShipBob is an great option for small businesses looking to save on their shipping costs while reducing shipping transit times.

We have been waiting for a wholesale order, it has been over 11 days, we have some presentation samples orders and we have to wait.

content.body: RUN! RUN AS FAST AS YOU CAN! I really do feel bad for you if you have already signed a contract with shipbob, this is the worst fulfillment center you will ever find. Hopefully you can find a way out before it's too late. I will say the company was not always this bad. 2 years ago Shipbob was smaller and they actually did a ton to care for their customers and they took pride in doing good work. Come to present day and they have been sitting on a container of our product for 6 days now and still haven't even stored it let alone shipped. We are literally begging for an update and all they do is send us links to their SLA. We get it, however, our agent promised us this was priority and turns out that was NOT the case. I guess this is what happens when you put profits over customer experience. Plus, its not just us look at all of the most recent reviews. To make matter worse their shipping rates are extremely expensive and they constantly lose product. NO JOKE, this year they lost an entire skew of ours and we had to tell our most important customer than none of his 17 clubs would get the product. He was blown away that a center could be so careless and he laughed at us when we said we used shipbob. He called them the " NIGHTMARE OF FULFILLMENT CENTERS" lol In closing I am writing this to all of the entrepreneurs out there considering shipbob as a partner. You have worked your butt off to get where you are, poured your heart into your brand, don't make the mistake of going with a company that will destroy your brand image. Go find a partner that will help your hard work pay off.

classification.summary: Terra-Core Fitness is actively leaving ShipBob due to severe service deterioration including 6-day storage delays, lost inventory, broken promises, expensive rates, and damaged customer relationships caused by the company prioritizing profits over customer care.

Save yourself time and money. ITs not even worth having there free plan, customer support is too bad , really bad, no contact for days, then don't answer your question, then dont fix the issue, i have literally just got my other e-commerce shop owners, all 27 shops to all switch from this too SHIPPING IS EASY.. so much better app ,and customer support the best . content.body: The app doesn't put import/export data for international shipments: hts code, values, etc. It is causes problems, because they ignored it. It is in their api and simple to include. There is no reason to leave it out. It reduces the value of the the app and service.

content.body: I removed it after struggling soo much with the shipping.. It won't ship anything, anywhere and it's just bad business for small and new stores who try to grow up! content.body: Truly awful development team removing or breaking features with every update. Every bug report is ignored. Every feature request is ignored. content.body: Shipbob has put our amazon account in jeopardy with a 16% late ship rate. Technical support is a carousel that goes nowhere. They have an online "chat" window, where the representative does basically nothing but open a support ticket - which go unanswered. Additionally, they sent boxes of assembly parts to a customer (who had no idea why he got these miscellaneous plastic rings). This led to us having to product to assemble, costing us revenue. Most importantly, just google "Shipbob" and "BBB" and read the number of Better Business Bureau horror stories about Shipbob. Or just look at reviews and see how horrific the stories are. The onboarding is very slick, they put a lot of energy into getting you on, but they can't get products out, nor do they offer technical support that is useful or helpful.

classification.summary: Merchant is actively switching away from ShipBob due to critical service failures including 16% late shipment rate, non-responsive technical support, and fulfillment errors that jeopardized their Amazon account and caused customer confusion.

content.body: This was the best app we had. Once it was updated IT IS COMPLETE TRASH!! Their support will take weeks with no help. A glitch a day will make you feel like you work for Ship station and not your own business.

content.body: Do not use shipstation if you plan on adding new products to your store on a regular basis. It seemed amazing until I found out that they require you to manually add information for every product you have such as weight, value, customs info and more...all info that was already added to shopify in the first place creating twice the amount of work. The workaround they gave me was to create a spreadsheet and re-import that every time I add a new product. Ridiculous.

classification.summary: Canadian merchant frustrated with ShipStation's requirement to manually duplicate product information (weight, customs data, etc.) already in Shopify, creating redundant work with only spreadsheet workarounds, and is actively looking to switch providers.

content.body: As a small business trying to expand globally, Easyship has been amazing at making international shipping headache-free. content.body: I worked with several fulfilment centers in my career and never had a worse experience than with ShipBob. Our company kept receiving complaints from DTC customers that were receiving products heavily stained, dented and damaged, covered in black dust that looked like soot. We placed a number of trial orders and had the same issue every single time, products arriving in horrible conditions. After asking ShipBob multiple times for an explanation, product audit and resolution, we waited months and finally received appalling pictures that showed our expensive products "stored" in haphazard mounds on top of each other in filthy boxes on the floor. It was evident from the pictures that many units were stained, dented, broken, leaked. We requested a report of damaged vs salable units and a credit for the damages. After over three months, we still have received no answers on the counts and were told that all of our damages are due to a mysterious "manufacturing defect" and Ship Bob is refusing to cover for any of it. These are thousands upon thousands of dollars of loss plus DTC customers being lost due to the poor experience. Further to this, the customer service is absolutely useless for any matter beyond the simplest things and will just provide pointless "cut and paste" answers that do not resolve your issues. I absolutely DO NOT recommend this company to anyone who values their business and brand equity. I wish I could attach the horrible pictures for everyone to see.

classification.summary: Mullein and Sparrow experienced severe product damage, poor storage conditions, thousands of dollars in losses, and inadequate customer service from ShipBob, with no resolution after three months, and is actively warning others against using the company.

content.body: Very happy with the Shopify app.It makes shipping a super breeze.Really a good app.I highly recommend you to try them out

content.body: If Zero starts was possible i will give it. Unless you are interested in ruining your business i will deeply suggest that you don't use shipbob. I read all the bad reviews online and still ignored it. Please don't be like me. Shipbob as completely ruined my business and left me with a chain of angry customers. For a whole month, all my international orders where shipped and automatically returned to sender. This is terrible because international shipping with shipbob costs a fortune. How can a business survive this. Let me explain how terrible this is. i spend $20 plus on ship an order that cost $60 including shipping fee. Shipbob ships the item unsuccessfully and then charge me $20 to return the item and now wants to charge me another $20 to reship the item. So basically i have lost $60 for every international order i got. That almost a $6000 dollar loss because this happened with close to 100 orders in one month. My terrible experience with shipbob has sent me into terrible depression. It has been the worst experience of my life. My paypal account and stripe is ruined because of multiple disputes due to lost packages and undelivered items. Please do not use shipbob, they will ruin your life and the business you've worked so hard to build.

content.body: Loyal ShipStation user for years, had gotten quite used to things and everything ran fairly smoothly. Had gotten the new layout forced upon me but always could switch back. It's really sad, I loved this stuff, and now it's borderline unusable, and searching for other solutions.

I will say it does seem like sometimes they will push new updates and sometimes those updates can cause the app to act a little buggy. content.body: ShipStation recently forced users into a new interface that is awful and buggy. It now takes much longer to get packages out the door and creating returns and return labels takes twice as long as it used to. Good luck getting anyone from ShipStation to respond to help requests. Actively looking for a replacement because of how slow it's made fulfilling orders.

classification.summary: DinkyDogClub is actively seeking a ShipStation replacement due to a buggy new interface that has significantly slowed their order fulfillment and return label creation processes, compounded by poor customer support responsiveness.

content.body: I've used this app for literal years and years, and it's been perfectly fine. Ever since they updated the layout and forced it on us with no option to revert it's been buggy, glitching out at anything more than a few orders at a time, and have completely disrupted the work flow many of us have been using for years. Now it's taking 2-3x as long to do the same basic stuff we used to do. Please use Pirate Ship or something else. Ship Station sucks

classification.summary: Long-time ShipStation user actively switching due to a forced UI redesign that introduced bugs and significantly slowed their order processing workflow, explicitly recommending Pirate Ship as an alternative.

content.body: I've been using Shipstation for about 5 years now, and this most recent update is absolutely horrible. They made it very hard to split orders, and the newly split orders no longer have their own order number. Now, as of July 1st, you can't even split orders anymore. I'm sure it's just a glitch, but when we have a large order that needs to be split into multiple shipments, we can't do it anymore. Printing labels now takes extra unnecessary steps. When you hover over an order, order details automatically pop up in a screen in front of you and doesn't go away until you click the popup away. That's annoying. They also took my custom layout for printing and wiped it clean with this new update, so I have to re-establish that. This new update is absolutely horrible, and good luck figuring out how to contact them. I am on the Gold plan and it took me 30 minutes to figure out how to contact them because help just says that if I'm on the gold plan, I should click the ? and a chat button will be there. There is no chat button. I actually have no idea what I clicked on in 30 minutes of searching to make the chat button appear, but it was ridiculous. This update will more than likely cause us to leave Shipstation after many years with them because each day it just gets worse.

classification.summary: Long-time ShipStation customer is extremely frustrated with recent updates that degraded order splitting functionality, print workflows, and UI usability, and is planning to leave the platform after 5 years due to declining product quality.

content.body: So many glitches. We have lost over $1000 in orders going incorrectly to the wrong people due to label glitches and another glitch where it would not mark an order sent so our team sent it twice. Definitely do not recommend.

classification.summary: Merchant from Australia experienced significant financial losses due to recurring glitches in order processing and shipping label generation, prompting them to actively seek alternatives.

content.body: When it is working fine, it is a treat to use. Integration with our Shopify store is a must, and it usually work great. Lots of features and integrations with carriers. Structurally, I have little to complain about. However, the app is glitchy sometimes. Various pieces stop working for short periods of time, although their "uptime meter" claims everything is A-OK. More importantly, however, their service has been getting sloooooooow. I've had 2 tickets open for days, and nobody has helped me yet. One of the tickets is essential to our business to be tended to immediately, yet... crickets. The service was always a bit lacking, but by this point they badly need more support staff.

classification.summary: ShipStation merchant frustrated with recent service slowdowns, unresponsive support tickets, and app glitches despite generally liking the product's features and Shopify integration.

content.body: Shipping orders themselves seem to be going alright, but I've been dumbfounded at the number of basic things we've asked for where the response has been "You're the first person to ever ask about this feature!" which I have a hard time believing. For example, printing one Order Summary per page when bulk printing is not an option (seriously?). The most recent shock has been that, in order to simply increase the font size of the packing slips/order summaries so our more elderly staff members can read them, I have to upgrade to a higher plan that unlocks "customization" of documents. I don't want to fully customize the documents, I just want the font bigger so our staff can read it. Sucks that they want to charge us an extra $240 per year for the privilege of printing readable documents. We're in too deep now to switch apps, but my advice to anyone considering this would be to ask all those "seemingly dumb" questions you have about features so you're not shocked about what's not included down the road.

classification.summary: ShipStation customer frustrated with missing basic features like per-page printing and font size adjustment, forced to pay $240/year extra just to make packing slips readable for elderly staff, but locked in and unable to switch.

content.body: We've been with ShipMonk for awhile now and they have been absolutely wonderful! When we first partnered with them they were a bit smaller and still somewhat new, since then they've grown into a larger 3PL company, with updated backend apps/software, more staff, more features, warehouses, better prices, etc. The backend software/dashboard is intuitive, constantly getting updated, it shows your whole business at a glance and is easy to see and edit your orders from a birds-eye-view. After the initial onboarding process, everything integrates perfectly with Shopify. We've personally had little to no problems with ShipMonk themselves, errors are usually caused by USPS or other shipping companies. When we do have an issue or concern, the "Happiness Engineers" at ShipMonk are phenomenal, they're your personal point of contact and you really establish a good relationship with these engineers, they can help you with anything you need to ensure things go smoothly and they respond very quickly. Overall, we're very happy with the service from ShipMonk. With any 3PL you will run into issues here and there, it's a complicated logistical system shipping millions of packages a year, but with ShipMonk, together we've ironed out the initial issues and have been smooth sailing to success!

content.body: Shipmonk has stupid high shipping. I have products that cost up to $100 to ship and they split it into two shipments AND charge the fulfillment fee for each. These fees are the same price as the storage fees so storage is double the price they say it is. Another thing about storage is that the allowable height of a pallet is on the lower side meaning they charge more than you can find elsewhere per pallet. When I started with them, they would reply to my emails and tickets within 24 hrs and now it takes them 4+ days. Many of the questions I ask them are for customers of mine (sometimes shipping delays and even not receiving). And then I often end up just giving my customers their money back While I wait for Shipmonk's reply. I found a company that has cheaper storage (larger pallets for same monthly fee) plus they don't charge the additional fulfillment fees AND they don't have a minimum monthly order. (The storage is literally half the price) And a HUGE advantage of this new company is the amazingly lower shipping cost. This $100 Shipping cost plus $9 fulfillment fees through Shipmonk is now only costing me a total of $30 with this new company - that includes fulfillment! (Went from $109 to $30) I unfortunately still have products in their warehouse and being charged a stupid amount. I have paid this other fulfillment company to pick them up. I will still need to pay Shipmonk a large amount on top of that for this other company to pick them up. Do not go with Shipmonk!!!

content.body: This review is primarily for the ShipHero 3PL software. Before moving to ShipHero, we were fulfilling 5K+ orders/mo very manually—no SKUs or inventory tracking, manual 3PL billing for other brands we fulfill for etc. We evaluated several 3PL solutions to help us scale fulfillment our own brands, as well as build out a new warehouse to grow our 3PL company. We finally settled on ShipHero because of the full suite 3PL software they've built rather than using separate apps for what we needed. We scored with Brett Oliveira initially, and then Mike Azimi as our onboarding specialist, and he was AMAZING to work with. The onboarding was super smooth, and we're now shipping 7K+ orders/mo for several brands from our first warehouse running ShipHero within a matter of weeks. Some specific things we really appreciate about ShipHero 3PL software: - Kits! We can create a kit SKU and assign multiple items to be picked/tracked as components in different locations in the warehouse.- The efficiency of the picking flow through the warehouse.- Replenishment reports for optimizing our team to restock pickable locations.- Using Purchase Orders for receiving inventory is super streamlined, and makes it easy for our team to be efficient.- The whole iOS/scanner implementation makes it easy to onboard new employees and make them productive immediately.- Custom orders statuses that we can use to have pickers focus on specific 3PL brands.- Order automation rules for adding SKUs to orders based on the product SKUs (e.g. adding ring box SKUs to ring orders, necklace pouches to necklace orders, etc.)- 3PL billing! We haven’t even scratched the surface yet of all the detailed levels of billing you can apply to your 3PL customers - per pick, n+ picks, bins used in the warehouse, cubic storage used, etc. GREAT STUFF!- There’s bound to be more things here as we explore the software further. The big selling point is that they use their own software in their own warehouses which means they dogwood their own features before providing them to us 3PL software customers. Before we started with ShipHero we visited their Allentown warehouse and got a ton of ideas for how to implement our warehouse layout and workflows. We still have yet to nerd out on Xbox Kinect for our packing stations, but they even have a KB article when we have some time :) We’d definitely recommend ShipHero if you’re looking to scale your 3PL company and want a SAAS solution from a growing company that is continually building features that help you grow with them.

classification.summary: Merchant scaled from 5K to 7K+ monthly orders within weeks using ShipHero's integrated 3PL software suite, praising its kits, picking efficiency, replenishment tools, PO management, mobile scanner implementation, custom order statuses, automation rules, and flexible billing options.

content.body: Shipstation is a fantastic platform for managing shipments! I've been using it for over a year and a half, and it continues to surpass my expectations. Pros: - The interface is nicely laid out to give access to all of the tools we need without being overwhelming, with good navigation and tab organization - You can set up customized filter and column views, and save them as tabs for easy repeat reference - The search function with advanced search is very accurate and reliable, with options to fine tune the results extensively - Product info can be updated with selective imports so you're not overwriting any existing data - There are multiple stackable options for product and order organization to give very granular control over your systems - The automation rules are extensive, with virtually every product and order variable usable as an automation trigger - Built-in batch fulfillment with the ability to generate batches through both automation and manual filtering: if you've set the item weights/boxes/customs info/batches/filtering/etc up correctly, you can process orders near-instantly - Networked printer integration: if you set up the connection with Shipstation Connect (free desktop application) on one computer, you can print from Shipstation on any device - Support is quick to respond and very knowledgeable - The documentation is extensive, easy to understand, and simple to navigate through - The pricing is also very affordable for the amount of time we save - It has integrations for all the platforms we use and can manage multiple stores and couriers The only notable con is that Shipstation currently lacks built-in tools for tracking packing materials: you can create custom box sizes and add them to orders seamlessly with both automatic and manual options, but you can't add a weight to a box listing and have the order weight adjust automatically when you change the box, or remove a box from inventory when you process a shipment. Small thing, but annoying: we end up adding the box weight to the product weights so we can batch fulfil our 1-2 item orders, but it messes all the calculations up for multi-item orders. Overall highly recommend, Shipstation is an excellent platform: I've recommended it to several small business owners in passing, and I'd recommend it in general to anyone who has to track orders.

content.body: I was really excited to work with Shipmonk, their service seemed really great. However after 1 year with them I'm canceling my contract because of the constant loss of inventory that has occurred. Pros: Easy to use integrated software that syncs with Shopify and also automatically downloads my Etsy orders. Inventory tracking feature that updated my Shopify stock. When everything went well, being a Shipmonk customer was great. Orders were automatically fulfilled and it saved hours of work. Cons: They lost thousands of dollars of inventory over the course of a year, a fraction of which was reimbursed. When you sign up for their service the terms and conditions say that even though they might be at fault for lost inventory, they are not financially responsible. It was incredibly frustrating having to email their customer service every week to report an inventory discrepancy. I sell unique clothing designs that have a high production cost so I suspect staff members were stealing my products each time an order was fulfilled. Their Happiness Engineers were always very apologetic each time I reported a claim, but failed to solve the issue or explain why my inventory continually went missing in their warehouses. The rare times that Shipmonk did agree to reimburse me, they only gave me a $10 credit per unit which was a small percentage of the production cost. The lost inventory has negatively impacted my business' reputation. Because of Shipmonk's errors, each month I had to message customers to apologize that their orders could no longer be fulfilled. Many of the missing products were irreplaceable. Their software syncing system would list products as in stock on my website, then suddenly change to an inventory level of 0 when the order was being fulfilled, changing the order status to backordered. Because of Shipmonk's negligence, I've lost thousands of dollars worth of sales plus thousands in lost inventory.

classification.summary: YoshiZen is actively canceling their ShipMonk contract due to persistent inventory losses totaling thousands of dollars, poor reimbursement policies, suspected theft by warehouse staff, and software glitches that damaged their business reputation and sales.

content.body: Scale your ecommerce business with Shipstation. Don't waste time manually creating labels and sending tracking info to your customers. content.body: I am writing this review as a last ditch attempt to get support after over 25 chats and emails dating back at least a year. From the start I have been using the print automation to print an Endicia Pay Upon Use Return Label when I print a shipping label. About a year ago the automation got a bug, and will randomly stop working. Once it stops, every order there after will not print the return label. I have over 25 chats and emails and was told my case is "escalated", but since it is not a wide spread issue there is no timeline when it will be fixed. The work around was to use the shipstation branded return portal. OK, I set it up. Now this is a work around, but it costs me more money. This is because I can only use 1 type of return. So if a package goes out USPS first class mail for $5, it has to come back using Priority Mail for $8. Not great, but hopefully it would get me by until the automation was fixed. About 2 months ago the returns portal stopped working. It now tells my customers their order has not yet shipped and no label can be produced. This case was also "escalated" but there is no date for it to be fixed because it is not a wide spread issue...so I am told. Now I spend way too much time dealing with returns because both options I have to automate this process are busted and I cant get anyone to fix one or the other. I get emails and calls from people needing return labels that I have to stop what I am doing and go manually produce the label. If these issues cannot be fixed in the near future I will be leaving shipstation for a competitor. I like everything else about shipstation, and when everything functions it meets my needs perfectly. But the support has been terrible, I keep getting the same scripted lines over and over, and my issues are no closer to being fixed than they were a year ago. Your move shipstation, your move.

classification.summary: JetSeam is actively threatening to leave ShipStation due to year-long unresolved bugs in return label automation and poor support responsiveness, forcing manual workarounds that increase costs and operational burden.

content.body: Pro: -Generally positive experience for orders placed and fully shipped within a few days. For orders with longer wait times, or orders that are split into multiple shipments we have some issues. -Automated completion of customs forms for international shipments. Con: -Long times between automated store synchronization: Can be over two hours between store updates from Shopify. There is the option to manually update the stores, but it is not feasible to manually update the store 24/7 if there are other processes that rely on timely information. -Poor support for split-shipped orders. If one item from an order ships, Shipstation will not process any edits to the order (e.g. an item added to the order, an item removed from the order, other quantity adjustments to the order, address edits or updates) -Orders only open to edits for a short time: After 60 days no order edits or changes are passed to Shipstation. With supply chain delays we regularly have orders that are older than 60 days that require changes or edits. -Long loading times in Shipstation: with orders that have a large number of items, or orders that have one or more previous shipments there is a VERY LONG and noticeable delay from the time an order is opened in Shipstation before the page fully loads and we are able to make adjustments in weight, shipping service, return labels, or print a label. -Unreliable USPS tracking numbers generated during peak seasons: Recently, large quantities of tracking numbers that are generated for our USPS shipments have not been reset before being reused. These tracking numbers provide the shipment history of shipments that were completed previously during the year, and we have been unable to find a solution to reset those so that our customers receive accurate tracking information for their order.

classification.summary: Wool&Prince experiences persistent operational friction with ShipStation's sync delays, split-shipment handling limitations, order edit windows, page load performance, and USPS tracking number reliability during peak seasons, creating pain points in their fulfillment workflow.

content.body: SHIPBOB Australia B2C Fulfilment ex Altona Warehouse - Absolute HORROR!We used shipbob for just under 6 months via WIX and Shopify Store. Originally attracted by what appears a solid and streamlined Tech Platform with experienced fulfilment providers for reasonable and transparent cost.Having experienced shipbob for months we offer our strong warning to store operators. Every Warehouse receiving order was either lost for 7 days and took a further 7-14 days until the stock was listed in inventory for dispatch. On one occasion the warehouse told the delivery contractor they will not accept anything via Shipbob which was the indication for us to make an urgent move.As to the actual picking and shipping of our orders their service appeared efficient and good (but our customers did not take kindly to excessive delays with receiving of good into the FC). We had countless email communication with the support team who seem to be the go-between melbourne warehouse and US operations team. Once they ran out of excuses they just started to ignore us which made it a nightmare for us as we had no information to give our customers. In general terms we would say Shipbob will be unlikely to survive in Australia and they present a real danger to any business that makes the mistake top rely on them. And in the end we lost many orders because of them and scored the anger of many customers whilst Shipbob did not feel the need to compensate us even with a dollar.

classification.summary: Australian merchant urgently switched from ShipBob after 6 months due to severe inventory receiving delays (7-14 days), unresponsive support, lost orders, and customer dissatisfaction, with no compensation offered.

content.body: Keep in mind that if you use an automatic discount, the final amount won't be right in the packing slip. We have to leave this app because our customers think they are being overcharged due to packing slips being wrong about amount paid. They don't offer any solution, just this: Automatic Discounts: A discount applied at checkout that does not require the customer to manually enter a code at checkout. ‘Automatic Discounts’ from Shopify do not import into ShipStation. These discounts are a different type of discount than the ‘Discount Codes’ that would import as a line item. This difference may be why you see a discrepancy between Order Total and Amount Paid. This is not very professional.

classification.summary: Merchant is leaving ShipStation because automatic Shopify discounts don't import correctly into packing slips, causing customers to think they're being overcharged, and the company offers no solution to this critical fulfillment problem.

content.body: Out of ShipStation, Shippo, and EasyShip, I have had the easiest experience getting started with EasyShip-- they are true to their name! The main things I needed to start were:1. Generating labels for 4x7 thermal printer2. Calculating live shipping rates at checkout in my Shopify store3. Clear setup of products and boxes for accurate shipping rates4. Ability to offer free shipping up to a certain amount #2 was especially huge for me as I did not want customers to get unexpected shipping prices, as I'm still learning how to ship things I am not confident yet in what my shipping rates were. I did a test shipment to myself and it was seamless, it's everything I could ask for right off the bat.

classification.summary: Merchant praising EasyShip for ease of setup and ability to generate thermal labels, calculate live shipping rates at checkout, and offer conditional free shipping without complex configuration.

content.body: Migrating to Easyship has been a boon for our business. Reliable live rates, excellent customer service and support for a diverse range of carriers and sales channels. It's streamlined our logistics process at Plyco and we're eternally grateful!

classification.summary: Plyco, an Australian merchant, is satisfied with Easyship's reliable live rates, customer service, and multi-carrier integration that has streamlined their logistics operations.

content.body: How switching to ShipHero transformed our business in 90 days My skincare brand BlackWolfNation.com was doing very basic in-house fulfillment using ShipStation. We grew from 20 orders a day to over 1000 in just 12 months and needed a more robust solution to continue growing. Working with Shiphero we were able to implement a receiving ASN system, warehouse locations, faster and more accurate pick, pack, and ship procedures, rate shopping, and setting up bundled components for accurate inventory. ShipHero is a very powerful out-of-the-box solution that paid for itself instantly. With the help of the Shiphero team, we then transformed our in-house fulfillment operation into ONE23 Fulfillment - a thriving 3PL business within 90-days. We now ship for many top DTC brands and are pushing out thousands of orders a day. This is an extremely scalable solution that continues to innovate and add new features. Onboarding new clients, portal access, and billing are all fantastic. Without ShipHero we never could have grown as fast as we have or had the confidence to implement it in the first place and get the most out of the platform. All I can say is we are thankful for ShipHero every day.

classification.summary: BlackWolfNation transformed their in-house fulfillment operation into a thriving 3PL business using ShipHero's scalable platform, enabling growth from 20 to 1000+ orders daily and now handling thousands of daily orders for multiple DTC brands.

content.body: I have been running businesses for 30+ years, and I can say, without a doubt, that no single business I have ever worked with has damaged the reputation of my company more than ShipMonk. Make no mistake, ShipMonk is a sales company, and cares very little for their customers once they have signed you up. I strongly urge you to go back through Shopify’s reviews from 2020 and early 2021. Recently, the reviews are all “miraculously” five-stars, and do not sound authentic….sound a bit more like advertisements than reviews. Who knows, maybe these vendors have had experiences with a ShipMonk from an alternate universe. And i would not buy the "COVID, COVID, COVID, we have addressed those issues" line. A Zebra does not change its stripes. Here are my experiences…judge for yourself and beware before getting in bed with these guys. Also check out their reviews on Google. All of my experiences outlined below are well-documented, and if you check out other reviews, you will see similarly bad experiences, though maybe not in as much detail! ShipMonk’s systems are poorly designed, their internal communications are terrible, their email system is very inefficient and frustrating to use, and their customer service is atrocious, both in terms of response time, getting accurate information and maybe most importantly, attitude. It is extremely slow…be prepared to wait sometimes days for a response, which is often unhelpful or inaccurate, and then wait again for their response to your response, etc., etc. Many of their customer service reps are poorly trained, often not knowing answers to basic questions or, worse, delivering incorrect information, and it is almost as if they have been trained to take any opportunity to blame the customer for any issues that arise. All of this adds up to a terrible customer experience, a lot of frustration and stress, and often requires hours each week to sort out their mess, all the while fielding phone calls from unhappy customers due to shipping errors and delayed shipments. A fulfillment center should be taking fulfillment issues off your desk, so you can focus on your business. With ShipMonk, hours a week are spent double checking their work or trying to get them to do what they are supposed to do on time and without being asked. I was with ShipMonk for about a year and a half. They initially flat out misrepresented their capabilities to me. I see from WAMA Underwear’s review, that ShipMonk is still habitually misrepresenting their services in order to sign up new clients. Like WAMA’s, their misrepresentation to us was not discovered, of course, until all of our inventory had arrived at their warehouse. They made very little effort, or displayed any urgency, to rectify the situation, and made no offer to reduce their cost of service, which went up substantially due to their misrepresentation of what they could and could not do, and at what price. All the while, their auto-payment systems keeps pulling funds from your account, while getting any kind of solution drags on and on. I gave them multiple opportunities, and tried to work with them, to make things right, but they were clearly not interested and could not be bothered. It seems their sole focus is signing up new clients, and not providing good service with a good attitude to the clients they have signed up. They have almost an adversarial relationship with their customers, as if you are just a necessary evil to generate revenue and grow. We make and sell an exercise systems. ShipMonk sent our customers, among other things, a dress, a purse, a sweatshirt and tap shoes…yes, tap shoes. They always send you a form apology email, letting you know how they are working to see that this does not happen again…until it happens again. Mispicks, missing items and wrong sizes were also a common problem. Their time estimates for special projects are not to be taken seriously. They almost never meet their own estimate, and instead, deliver excuse after excuse. You cannot rely on their status updates to communicate with your customers, because you have no idea if they will do what they said they will do when they said they will do it, leading to upset customers who rightfully blame you. The costs they charged us for special projects were grossly over-charged, and only after I discovered them and brought it to their attention, did they acknowledge as much. To this day, it is not clear to me whether we were ever refunded the overcharges, and we spent months, yes months, just trying to establish a fair charge for the work (we decided to leave before that was resolved). They provide very little transparency in regards to their costs and pricing…you have to dig into their reporting for the information. Though they have many shipping options, they are rarely the most cost effective. Since leaving, we have found significantly cheaper and more cost-effective rates, faster shipping and improved reliability. The attractive shipping options they quote are before carrier surcharges, which can be as much as 50-80% of the shipping cost itself. They partnered with several carriers during our time with them, who were flat out incompetent, lost packages, did not provide adequate tracking, etc. And ShipMonk will not allow you to follow up on your own to get tracking updates from carriers directly, so you must suffer through waiting for ShipMonk to follow up, which takes up valuable time and rarely delivers any actionable information. ShipMonk does not seem to understand, or more likely does not really care, that they are representing you, their customer, in the marketplace. When they screw up, or take days to get back to you, or ship tap shoes to your customers, the customer does not blame ShipMonk…they only know that you screwed up their order. Such is life with ShipMonk. I hope you find this information valuable. I thought it was important to share my experience in detail with you, to expose what I believe to be a very poorly run and untrustworthy company in the fulfillment space. There are plenty of fulfillment companies in the marketplace, and if you do your homework, you can likely find ones who pride themselves on customer service, and doing their job competently as an integral part of your business. And if something does go wrong, will give it and you the attention you deserve. In our experience, ShipMonk is not one of those companies. They are a sales company trying to grow as fast as they can, and you, as their customer, are of little consequence.

classification.summary: Long-time business operator severely damaged by ShipMonk's poor systems, terrible customer service, slow response times, and untrained staff; actively warning others and appears to have already left or is actively seeking alternatives.

content.body: We've been using EasyShip for a couple of months. Worldwide shipping is complicated, and they made it easy for us. Application is great for handling daily sales and fulfilling batch orders (i.e., Kickstarter campaigns). It has multiple functionalities we can't live without. To name a few:* Seamless, one-click integration with Shopify (you can add many stores)* Shipping at Checkout (your customer can choose a courier, and the shipping cost is accurate). It works worldwide!* Insurance* Shipping rules (a great tool to automate shipping scenarios for different locations or box sizes)* Product listing * Simple import from file (excellent for batch orders - you can import any CSV file, and EasyShip support many e-commerce platforms * Customizable notification emails to customers In addition, EasyShip provides excellent customer and technical support (helps with couriers, customers, and the application). We highly recommend this application and other EasyShip services.

content.body: Shipstation is great. It provides all sorts of ways to help us organize our workflow in our retail small business. We do lots of pre-orders, local pickup, and have many different staff with hands on our product. Being able to tag orders and comment within the program drastically helps our workflow. The ability to connect a scale directly to the software and weigh items is key. Plus, we can compare different carrier rates instantaneously to determine the lowest cost. With the ability to scan/verify orders via Shopify barcodes to the easy interface and ability to sort to their guaranteed best rates (I've compared), Shipstation provides the best and most robust shipping software out there.

content.body: I've used several shipping softwares and none of them have had as many features and customization as ShipHero. While we do run into some glitches here and there, it's because they are constantly working to improve the software. They are quick to resolve any issues and really listen to feature requests. The connection to Shopify is incredibly quick and accurate. This software has vastly improved our inventory management and fulfillment. The care that went into our onboarding process can't be beat. Our onboarding specialist made sure we were able to transition our processes as seamlessly as possible and helped tailor the system to our shipping requirements. Thank you Mike & ShipHero!

classification.summary: Holy Grail Steak Co. praises ShipHero's extensive features, customization capabilities, responsive support, and seamless Shopify integration that improved their inventory management and fulfillment operations.

content.body: We use ShipStation along with Shopify for shipping of our online orders. The integration is seamless and it's easy to compare shipping rates for different shipping partners like USPS, FedEx, UPS, etc. to find the most economical option or quickest delivery time. The labels are easy to print and the tracking numbers are helpful when we need to follow a package thru the delivery system. Even adding more postage to our online account is a breeze. If I could suggest anything, it would be to offer a subscription level between the 50 and 500 shipments per month level as we often exceed 50, but don't come anywhere near 500 per month.

classification.summary: Boys Town Gift Shop is satisfied with ShipStation's Shopify integration and shipping rate comparison features, but requests a mid-tier subscription plan between 50 and 500 shipments per month to better match their usage.

content.body: ShipStation is awesome, it's made a huge impact in the efficiency of our fulfillment. We use it with multiple sales channels, not just shopify, and have been very happy. The only reason for 4 stars (instead of 5) is because I couldn't find an easy way to feed live rates from Shipstation to our Shopify store based on dimensions and weight. Maybe I missed it somewhere, but we'd like customers to just pay actual shipping costs and instead we have estimates based on size/weight buckets.

classification.summary: Merchant is very satisfied with ShipStation's multi-channel fulfillment efficiency but wants better integration for live shipping rate calculations based on package dimensions and weight to pass actual costs to customers.

content.body: We've been using Shipstation for 3 years, shipping around 1000-1500 packages per year. The switch to Shipstation from manual shipping through the USPS was a quantum leap forward in efficiency. We couldn't run our business without Shipstation. Shipstation is perfect in nearly every regard except one: they aren't integrated fully with their parent company, Stamps.com. USPS international shipping options are not named the same in Stamps.com and Shipstation, which leads to confusion. I spent a few hours working with Shipstation user support to deal with the situation and it still wasn't resolved. I wish that Shipstation would change their shipping options to match the names that are officially used by USPS and Stamps.com. It's still not clear to me if Shipstation is permitting me to purchase all of the shipping options offered through my Stamps.com account (which was set up through Shipstation).

classification.summary: Long-time ShipStation customer praises the platform's efficiency but remains frustrated by inconsistent USPS international shipping option naming between ShipStation and parent company Stamps.com, creating confusion and unresolved support issues.

content.body: We have been using Easyship for over 6 months and it is by far the best shipping app we have ever used! And trust me, we have worked with over 7 major shipping services now to try and find the right fit for our business! There are many reasons we have finally found our home with Easyship, but first and foremost the user interface is beautiful and functional, and the customer service is amazing! We love the integrated shopping cart with customized carrier options for every customer, the ability to offer shipping insurance, and the focus on international carrier options. In the backend, we love that we can ship in bulk, while easily changing weight or label options for an individual order without leaving the page. It is also incredibly easy to create custom shipping rules, and the filtering options are almost unlimited! Hands down, this is the best shipping app we have ever used! :)

classification.summary: Femallay Inc. is highly satisfied with Easyship after testing 7+ other shipping services, praising its user interface, customer service, bulk shipping capabilities, and customization features.

We also had the opportunity to meet Mike for an in-person training where he helped our team get up and running with the entire shipping process. After just a few weeks navigating inventory counts and bulk uploads, we’re seeing huge benefits from ShipHero and we’re on-track with our shipments processing smoothly. Big thanks to Mike and the ShipHero team for all the amazing support!

Even after just a few weeks, we're already starting to see huge benefits from using ShipHero and are looking forward to growing even more with ShipHero's help!

Based on marketing emails and recommendations from various business-related sites, I've looked into other companies providing similar services, but I haven't seen any that provide services or lower costs that would justify making the change.

We've been able to personalize the shipping experience from our multiple Shops through ShipStation, It's also helped us choose the most efficient shipping mode and save money on our shipping spend. We also utilize ShipStation for our returns program by sending shipping labels for RMA requests. I would highly recommend ShipStation for others looking for shipping options for Shopify Shops.

Firstly the help was second to none, thank you Joe! The early results are looking good and I'm just waiting on the right details to integrate my own carriers. I feel this will give my customers a better choice of carrier, and hopefully reduce my abandon cart rates. *** Updated - Joe disappeared and I now find the shipping rules inadequate to filter out simple needs. There also seems no way to deal with the new EU IOSS tax system.
