content.body: ShipStation has saved me HOURS a week in manual shipping entries. The automation rules alone have been tremendously helpful and the ability to customize labels is an added bonus. Great customer support, I'll be a happy customer for as long as I own an ecommerce business.

classification.summary: Merchant is highly satisfied with ShipStation's automation rules, label customization, and customer support, reporting significant time savings and long-term loyalty.

content.body: I started using shipstation about 3-4 years ago and love the integration between shopify and shipstation. You can do stand alone shipments through shipstation; as well as shipstation can give you reports on cost of shipping between any storefront used. This is my go to program and shipping rates are great and the best deals possible on ups when I can ship a package for $10 compared to $16 on other programs. Highly recommended for sure!

classification.summary: Long-time satisfied customer praising ShipStation's Shopify integration, reporting functionality, competitive shipping rates, and superior UPS pricing compared to competitors.

content.body: Stamps.com owns Shipstation and likes to make a sizeable margin on the USPS Priority Mail labels they offer through Shipstation. Do your own comparisons on rates for your shipping needs before settling on any label and shipping solution for your business -- we realized that the full cubic rates that are available to us through Shopify or Shippo are always much less expensive than what Shipstation/Stamps.com is willing to provide to small businesses that do not quality for a bulk high-volume direct contract with USPS. Likewise the UPS Air and Ground rates that were offered to us with our own UPS account through Shipstation were always significantly more than the same UPS rates available to us through Shopify or Shippo. The Shipstation UI, bulk order processing and printing, the ability to print to a remote printer with Shipstation Connect, and the customer self-service returns portal were all decent and useful over the eight years we were a Shipstation customer. There is even a smartphone app for on-the-go order processing.

classification.summary: Seattle Thread Company switched away from ShipStation after 8 years due to significantly higher shipping rates (USPS Priority Mail cubic rates and UPS rates) compared to alternatives like Shopify and Shippo, despite appreciating the platform's UI and features.

content.body: App works perfectly for our needs. We have been using Shopify for years. We specifically like the item view, where you can see a thumbnail of what the customer ordered. We also like the feature that allows you to see if this is a new customer or a repeat buyer. They are attentive to customer issues, have very little down time and over all it is the best out there- (we have looked)

classification.summary: Nature's Fabrics praises ShipStation's intuitive interface, customer visibility features, and reliable support after years of use and comparison with alternatives.

content.body: I like using Shipstation as standalone software but this app is just not cutting it. It's incredibly buggy and inconsistent in the rates its showing. It's super frustrating as a merchant to not be able to offer consistant and reliable live rates to our customers. Customer service is incredibly difficult to get.

classification.summary: Emily's Chocolates is frustrated with ShipStation's app reliability and rate inconsistencies that prevent offering accurate live shipping rates to customers, and finds their customer service difficult to access.

content.body: Easyship has some of the best customer service I have experienced. To illustrate, we once had the problem that we had to manually add the shipping details for our shipments from Easyship into the shopify order. After telling our customer success manager about this they programmed a solution for us within two weeks free of charge that solved the problem. They have proven to always be this responsive, no matter what the request is, and this is what makes Easyship worth the slightly higher price than for example Amazon, who would never bother to do anything with your request. It truly seems that Easyship want your business to succeed. They have also helped us in establishing a new warehouse, and resolved problems with our couriers. Would definetly recommend them to anyone looking for someone who actually does something with your requests for help. Easyship feels more like a partner than a service.

classification.summary: Norwegian merchant praising Easyship's exceptional customer service and responsiveness, highlighting custom solutions and partnership approach that justify premium pricing compared to Amazon.

content.body: We have been using Easyship for several years and the experience has been very good. Essentially the app allows any business or person to connect with their large network of couriers, both local and international - allowing one to send packages internationally at reasonable rates. The app is well designed and easy to use. Love how seamlessly it syncs up with Shopify, customers can see the many shipping options at checkout, delivery lead time, estimated taxes/customs incurred - everything is presented succinctly. For a business, this app has been instrumental in connecting us to the rest of the world. The app continues to work on bringing added features and it is also worth to mention the tremendous customisable options on the app. Customer service has been quite prompt, more importantly our Customer Success Manager, Calvin, has been stellar and goes above and beyond to provide support and care. His genuine care for his clients definitely gives us greater confidence in the app and its services moving forward. Highly recommended!

classification.summary: Singapore-based merchant praising Easyship's multi-courier network, seamless Shopify integration, reasonable rates, customization options, and excellent customer success support after years of successful use.

content.body: I can't imagine doing business without ShipBob. I recommend them to everyone I meet in the eCommerce industry. The customer service is incredible, even though I rarely need it. I go weeks without even checking to see if everything is shipping properly as I've had so few issues. ShipBob is the best!

classification.summary: Hibernation Sleep Formula is highly satisfied with ShipBob's fulfillment service and reliable customer support, requiring minimal intervention and actively recommending the provider to others.

content.body: Ship Bob does a good job for us. The interface is pretty easy to use and they ship on time. I would recommend them

classification.summary: Positive review of ShipBob: Ship Bob does a good job for us. The interface is pretty easy to use and they ship on time. I would recommend them

content.body: We started using this app and it seemed great interface wise. The canadian courier prices were pretty high so it wasnt giving us the lowest rates, shopify shipping was still cheaper even though it states we get discounted rates. I've been trying to add our custom Canada post courier account, and its been like pulling teeth, the support team keeps giving me the run around.. and literally in the middle of a live chat session the support person said they had to run to a meeting and ended the chat.. so unprofessional.. what a waste. The platform is good but that is easily the worst support Ive come across and I have seen some bad ones.

classification.summary: Canadian merchant frustrated with Easyship's uncompetitive courier pricing, inability to integrate custom Canada Post account, and extremely poor customer support, actively seeking alternatives.

content.body: ShipMonk has been a really great partner with excellent customer service. Like everywhere, last year with the pandemic and then a blow out November/December, and then lingering effects of COVID-19 there were several unavoidable issues and delays but it is very clear ShipMonk had the leadership and strategy on how to come out of it and put in a lot of new tactics to make sure they are in a better position next year. They are continually improving and making things better which is something important with any partner. We recommend them!

classification.summary: Dang Foods praises ShipMonk's excellent customer service and continuous improvements, particularly their effective management through pandemic-related disruptions and capacity challenges.

content.body: Good platform and worked perfectly. Provide worldwide shipping without worrying about wrong shipping fee to our end or buyer. Customer support has been excellent in provide assist and fast response.

classification.summary: Positive review of Easyship: Good platform and worked perfectly. Provide worldwide shipping without worrying about wrong shipping fee to our end or b

content.body: My orders are totally automatic. Everytime I get a new order on my Shopify website the order is automatically fulfilled. Customer service on ShipBob is solid too. I don't have to wade through the endless amounts of complex, confusing steps and flaky cryptic customer service like with Amazon! I'm so relieved I don't have to have store my products and ship them out manually. Plus, when I do get an order from a customer on Amazon FBM (fulfillment by merchant) I have ShipBob do the fulfillment as well. Your customer, most likely, wont receive your product as quickly as Amazon, but it's really nice having my products with ShipBob where I can easily access it and be able to have a ShipBob rep help with all things fulfillment.

classification.summary: Merchant praising ShipBob's automatic order fulfillment and reliable customer service compared to Amazon FBA and manual fulfillment alternatives.

content.body: Love the customer service and the amount of support I received during my onboarding process! I'm anxiety-prone especially when it comes to getting started with something new and ShipMonk has been a great help...plus good vibes all around! The software is easy to use as well and has everything I need.

classification.summary: Canadian merchant praising ShipMonk's exceptional customer service during onboarding, user-friendly software, and positive company culture.

content.body: This was the first 3PL we have partnered with. We initially chose to work with ShipMonk because of their experience and infrastructure with Shopify and their ability to deal with mass quantities of orders. Their onboarding process was simple and a straightforward plug-in through the app store. The App interface is very well made and has some great resources for training purposes. We worked with Kerry who has gone above and beyond in communication and transparency. Their whole team of Happiness Engineers is a major highlight of our experience. On the ground their operations exceeded our expectations. We had 5 figures in order quantity, and they maintained an error margin of around .01%. All orders were fulfilled within 2-5 days keeping our customers extremely happy. Some minor things that we would like to see improved on:Accurate up-to-date inventory counts after fulfilled orders.A more straightforward "assisted" claim process for orders with errors. Even more visibility into the receiving process.Quicker updates to billing cycle and pricing changes. Overall, we would recommend to any company who is looking for custom fulfillment specialists. Just be clear to set expectations early, and ShipMonk will deliver.

classification.summary: A Ma Maniere is satisfied with ShipMonk's infrastructure, onboarding, service quality, and operations team, though they note minor opportunities for improvement in inventory accuracy, claims processes, receiving visibility, and billing updates.

content.body: Horrible experience... Stay far away! Initially, they (Erik van Bilderbeek, Jennifer Santanna & Kerry Neysmith) promised us they can do everything we asked. We sent 50% of our inventory to them. Only to find out after they received our products that their system doesn't allow them to do so. Before launching we placed test orders to ourselves to see if Shipmonk can fulfill correctly and each time they made unacceptable mistakes. We sell underwear and they literally had measured some of our underwear products with a 7 inch height. Actual height is about 0.3". Shipmonk also weighed our products incorrectly adding a 1 pound 8 ounces weight for an underwear. Actual weight is 0.2 lbs. These measurements and weights were mistakes after our complaints getting their manager, Kerry, involved and re-measuring everything. How can they be so off? How can they be so negligent!? After 3 months of consistent issues after issues we just couldn't continue with them. 3 months of time lost. Thousands of dollars lost with Shipmonk even though all the issues were clearly theirs. They do not own up to their own mistakes. We ended up having to pay for their negligence. Highly Not Recommended! Stay Far Away! Find Another Fulfillment Center!

classification.summary: WAMA Underwear is actively switching away from ShipMonk after 3 months of severe fulfillment errors (incorrect measurements and weights) and poor accountability that cost them thousands of dollars.

content.body: I'm still finding bugs on a weekly basis. Plus, whenever they push down an update, there's no notification. You never know when a new version is going to overwrite the old (it's web based). Nor is there anywhere you can go and find out what the latest bug fixes were. Using it is a big productivity boost, but it's simply unreliable. You don't dare print out more than 10 labels at a time, and you'd better count them as they print, to make sure none are skipped. Their current bug, is their integration with Amazon FBA. Shipstation simply refuses to see my FBA inventory accurately, resulting in me either submitting orders to Amazon FBA when there is no stock, or Shipstation will tell me that FBA has no stock, when indeed they do. They have a slick product, but they simply have very poor quality control in place, for the software updates. June 28/2021Well, here we are about a year later....Shipstation no long has the issue of printing incorrect label counts. Seems they fixed that. However, they still don't have a handle on the FBA integration. SS still reports your FBA quantities wrong, and SS also reports the FBA postage fee wrong (but only sometimes).

classification.summary: Long-time ShipStation user frustrated by persistent software quality issues, particularly unreliable FBA inventory integration and poor update communication, despite acknowledging some core productivity benefits.

content.body: Shipstation is great for keeping orders from many stores in one place. Great customer service too, very responsive!

classification.summary: Trixie Cosmetics praises ShipStation for its ability to consolidate multi-store orders and responsive customer service.

content.body: We love using ShipMonk their customer service is the best and with dedicated "happiness engineers" there are real people that can help solve fullfillment issues. They are an extention of our customer service and we appreciate their quick response times and attention to detail.

classification.summary: HAY! Straws praises ShipMonk's exceptional customer service, dedicated support team, and quick response times that help them resolve fulfillment issues effectively.

content.body: Easyship is quite a good platform as we can ship to plenty of countries worldwide without worrying about no shipping solution to destination country. content.body: We moved to Shipmonk due the increase in volume that we were doing along with the analytics they have to offer. The communication has been well with Michaela, Kerry, and Carmen and they are always happy to help. There is a lot of self education with the software but it is typically user friendly. They were able to get 7000 orders our in 2 days for us. The only charge that is difficult to track is the additional surcharges, residential delivery fee, and fuel surcharges. These sometimes add up but they are always happy to talk it through. Very pleased thus far!

classification.summary: Frost Buddy migrated to ShipMonk to handle volume growth and values the analytics, responsive customer service, and fast fulfillment speed, though they note some difficulty tracking additional surcharges.

content.body: I absolutely love ShipMonk! Their seamless integration with the Shopify cart is what led me to them. From my first call to ask questions about the service, to the onboarding process these guys made me feel like I had a team working with me to get my product up on the market. My Fulfillment Consultant Brandon was there every step of the way to answer my questions even the stupid ones. Not once was I pushed off to an FAQ or a link to go find the answers on my own. ShipMonk is not just a fulfillment house; they are an addition to your business team. So if you're on the fence and searching through reviews to make sure you're making the right decision like I was, trust these are the guys you've been looking for.

classification.summary: Merchant highly satisfied with ShipMonk's Shopify integration, personalized onboarding support, and team-oriented fulfillment service approach.

content.body: Shipbob has been a great fulfillment partner as we grew our business by over 300% last year. They continued to work with us amidst our growth and worked hard to meet our demand. On the functional side, orders get synced from Shopify easily and it really runs itself after integration. My one caveat is live shipping costs from CartBob. Intuitive in some ways, but not smart enough to handle our specific needs. Overall, excellent domestic 3PL service with great customer service and a growing international presence.

content.body: Shipbob does all our fulfillment for Shopify, Amazon, and Walmart stores, and they do a great job across all four warehouses we are using at Shopbob. content.body: Shipbob has been a great partner since we joined them a year ago. They literally saved us from a disastrous situation with a different 3PL group (I won't name names, but "RH" should give you a hint). Their support is great and quick to respond. The rates are fair. I particularly enjoy the "all-in" pricing model. And their growing network of warehouse locations, especially internationally, is really helping us to seamlessly expand our overseas operations. I would give 4.5 stars if I could. My main criticism has to do with the Shopify integration, specifically the warehouse locations. Currently, the integration is set up where ShipBob acts as the fulfillment service and does not support multiple warehouse locations. So whether you have 1 Shipbob location, 4 Shipbob locations, or multiple locations between Shipbob and other providers (we sometimes ship from our headquarters, for example), Shopify groups it all into one and really limits what you can see and do in Shopify. I've been told this is being addressed, so really looking forward to seeing this improved.

classification.summary: AKILA praises ShipBob's support, pricing, and international warehouse expansion, but notes frustration with limited Shopify integration for multi-location fulfillment visibility.

content.body: We use ShipBob for order fulfillment. They are a self service fulfillment operation, so as long as you feel comfortable navigating these waters with minimal support, I'd recommend them. However, after you reach a certain volume and $$ threshold, you get dedicated support. We've found the dedicated support very helpful. They just started offering 2-day delivery in the lower 48 states, so that was a big plus. We were considering using Deliverrr for the 2-day option, but they started offering this option before we made the switch. Sometimes standard shipping options are extremely delayed or never show up, (they use UPS mail innovations or DHL surepost for their standard options) but this might also happen elsewhere. International service offerings exist for Canada UK and EU. We also sell in Canada with ShipBob. As of now, they only offer Canada Post for standard and FedEx for expedited shipping. Very expensive options. The app itself works properly for us, although it would be nice to have ShipBob update inventory within Shopify. Perhaps we just haven't found this functionality yet.

classification.summary: Satisfied ShipBob customer praising dedicated support and new 2-day delivery option while noting minor pain points with standard shipping delays and expensive international shipping to Canada.

content.body: Love shipbob! Best 3pl out there no doubt, very organized, user friendly and great support. I definitely recommend.

content.body: Big thanks to ShipBob especially the customer support we've received since launching on their platform. It's a vast improvement from our previous distributor. Highly recommend.

classification.summary: Merchant is satisfied with ShipBob's platform and customer support, noting significant improvement over their previous distributor, and recommends the service.

content.body: Wouldn't Have Made over $3,000,000 Last Year Without Shipmonk! This software has expedited the SALES GROWTH of my business by removing the headache of shipping, storing, and hiring staff to package and process orders. With this weight off of my shoulders, I can 100% focus on new product development and effective marketing to continue increasing my business' revenue. The thing I LEAST LIKE about this software is that I didn't know it existed YEARS before! I have been using this software since the summer of 2018. Before that, I was STRUGGLING to keep up with packaging hundreds of monthly orders. Fast forwards years later, we now have THOUSANDS of orders each month. Image if I was still self-packaging my own orders with our current volume. This software is a MUST HAVE for serious business owners that want to build a business that can fully operate on its own. Overall, this software is CRITICAL because it frees my time, gives me location independence to travel, and enjoy my family without worrying about packaging material, processing orders, or interacting with shipping companies. This software gives me the security and confidence knowing that I am building a sustainable business that can and does run without me needing to be present for every aspect.

classification.summary: Long-time ShipMonk user credits the platform with enabling $3M+ revenue growth by automating fulfillment operations and freeing time for product development and marketing.

Unfortunately for us, our products are fragile, heavy, and parcel only, and the B2B services wasn't a good match. content.body: After several years we are ending our relationship with Shipbob. They have overcharged us multiple times to the tune of thousands of dollars. Most recently, we submitted a work order to remove our few remaining items so we could close out our account. After paying for that work order, they "found" two cases of our most popular product of which we'd been sold out of for months. We relisted the item on our website and sold all but two units just in case there were any lost/damaged orders. We then submitted a disposal request for the warehouse to dispose of the two remaining 3.4 fl oz tubes of moisturizer so we could close our account. This should only cost a few dollars. Instead, Shipbob charged us $500 to dispose of two skincare products that fit in the palm of your hand. We raised the issue with Shipbob and weeks later we are still awaiting the refund...

classification.summary: Merchant is terminating their relationship with ShipBob due to chronic overcharging and a disputed $500 disposal fee for two small items, with an unresolved refund request pending for weeks.

content.body: We love this app. It has saved us so much time in doing our shipping. We love how quickly we can do shipping for all our orders in just a few clicks.

classification.summary: Positive review of ShipStation: We love this app. It has saved us so much time in doing our shipping. We love how quickly we can do shipping for all our

content.body: I would highly recommend not using ShipMonk. I'm providing 2 stars because their UI is great, and the sync with Shopify is helpful so that we in theory don't have to update our inventory on a consistent basis (which in reality is not the case). If you have under 100 SKU's they may be a fit, we've been using them for a few years and everything was fine until we really starting scaling our inventory levels and SKU count. Then came receiving issues, lost inventory (and by default overselling to our customers and having to cancel orders), and sending the wrong product to our customers - as in not even inventory sold from our store (we sell dog merch, our customers have received human supplements, perfume, etc). I had to track down and reach out to the other stores separately to make sure they were aware their product (sometimes worth $100+) was being sent out to the wrong customers so they could also file claims. The lost inventory has been the biggest headache for us. When I say lost inventory I mean hundreds of units (some SKU's have a >6% shrink which ShipMonk claims is normal) that have gone missing within a few months, and no reimbursement with often what I would consider odd rational - they couldn't store everything in a single bin, therefore it's not their fault. Money drain! I will say different from other ShipMonk reviews I have mostly had good experiences with their CX team, and I think the bigger issue is that there are larger wide scale systemic issues with ShipMonk and the CX team is either not equipped to handle or they truly have no idea what happened and that's that. My negative experiences have been when agents will resolve a ticket when the issue is not fixed, so I've had to reopen tickets just to make sure they actually respond. Overall - I would keep shopping, not worth it.

classification.summary: Rover Store has experienced critical operational failures at ShipMonk including lost inventory (>6% shrink rates), wrong products shipped to customers, and overselling issues after scaling SKU count, and is actively looking to switch providers despite previously having a good experience.

content.body: i love it until it goes down or they do updates in the middle of working.for some reason i don't know why it does not pull down my shopify sales sales like it should. sometimes it does sometimes it doesn't. today shipstation pulled in a canceled sale from shopify, that was curious. other than my couple issues i love shipstation take a lot of work load off of me and helps me from making mistakes.

author.company: Hook, Line & Tinker - modern embroidery kits

content.body: I have been a Shipstation user for more than 2 years. When I switched my websites (I have different sites for wholesale and retail) over to Shopify last fall, the transition was seamless - even for my slightly less than simple setup. All my orders move into Shipstation from Shopify with one click. No manual order entry, and I have the flexibility to use my own commercial shipping discounts. Even my Etsy and Faire orders move readily into Shipstation. Easy peasy, and super fast.

classification.summary: Long-term ShipStation user praising seamless Shopify integration, fast order processing, and flexibility to use commercial shipping discounts across multiple sales channels.

content.body: We've been using shipstation for years! There are other shipping software options out there but Shipstation has never let us down. Great software, great company.

classification.summary: Long-time ShipStation customer praising the software's reliability and the company's consistent performance over years of use.

content.body: Their Monday morning outages are getting old. Happens way too often. Also, when you get customers, whose shipping labels show to have been paid for and printed, contacting you on a regular basis you will realize that you have to babysit this company's product. There are several threads on the Amazon Seller forum describing this exact issue, charging for shipping labels that aren't printed out. The latest glitch had occurred for over a week when using Shipstation Connect. Randomly printed out blank pages instead of Packing Slips. This seemed to occur when printing large batches. After waiting on their Chat for over an hour their poorly trained support person was zero help. Told us we should uninstall and reinstall the app & also tried to infer that it was our printer causing the problem. We have 2 printers and they both did the exact same skips. Another waste of time. We have found a workaround to avoid the missing packing slips. It takes more time but we can't continue having orders not shipped. Looking for a new option that isn't part of Shipstation or Stamps.com (the parent company). The fact that Shipstation took three weeks to respond to this clearly represents their approach to customer support.

classification.summary: WELdental is actively seeking to switch away from ShipStation due to recurring outages, chronic printing/label issues, poor support responsiveness, and lack of confidence in the platform's reliability.

content.body: Fantastic app. Is helping us break down the barriers when it comes to scaling an eCommerce business. Their Bulk shipping feature is a necessity for brands scaling around a hero product. Onboarding is incredibly easy and painless, our representative, Mike Azimi was a pleasure to work with and maybe one of the fastest communicators via email I've met.

classification.summary: Kong Beer Bong praises ShipHero's bulk shipping capabilities and ease of onboarding as critical tools for scaling their ecommerce business, with particular appreciation for their responsive account representative.

content.body: We absolutely love ShipStation! We have multiple shopify stores and Amazon accounts as well, the ability to sync it all into one software is a game changer! Highly recommended if you want to make shipping easy. ShipStation also has a deal with USPS to purchase postage at good rates as well. A+++++

classification.summary: Merchant is highly satisfied with ShipStation's ability to consolidate multiple Shopify stores and Amazon accounts into one platform, along with favorable USPS postage rates.

content.body: This is perhaps among the best apps out there. We can't function without it. Not just the connections and level of service but how versatile it is. We can control our sales from our phone app, or desktop, and has all the platform connections we need.

content.body: A serious lifesaver for managing all my shipments! This really helps eliminate any surprises our international customers may be hit with on delivery and improves conversions at checkout. I also love that it works so seamlessly with our shipping carriers and that we are able to easily import our discounted rates we negotiated with carriers. To add to this, their customer service is A++++! I worked with Hiromi and she was so wonderful throughout the whole process. Anytime I had a question, it was answered within 30 minutes. She also took the time to video conference with me and walk me through the whole platform and help me set it up! Thank you thank you thank you!

content.body: I've been using shipstation for about 3 years. While works well, as you grow you see it's limitations quickly. I went from 3 shipments a day to 60 and I can honestly say, I'm not impressed. Lacking features: 1. doesn't automatically pick the lowest shipping rate if you have multiple courier accounts; 2. fails to pick up HS codes for international shipping; 3. may not pick up sale prices and country of origin for customs. Those are my 3 biggest pet peeves.

classification.summary: High-growth merchant frustrated with ShipStation's limitations in automation (shipping rate selection, HS codes, customs data) as order volume scaled from 3 to 60 shipments per day.

content.body: Don't even waste your time... I spent about 5 hours trying to get this app set up so I could offer calculated international shipping rates with duty quote during checkout. This included importing CSV file with all my product sizes and easyship classifications, adding all my shipping container sizes one-by-one and then paying over $800 for the annual subscription to Shopify as well as chatting with Shopify to manually turn on 3rd party calculated rates. After all that, I tried adding a couple items to the cart and checking out... it worked! But then, I tried adding a few more items and whenever I got over 10 items, the shipping calculation would say "Your cart has been updated and the items you added can't be shipped to your address. We don't offer shipping to United Kingdom" (or whatever country you might be testing) HUH?!? This is probably the worst wording imaginable. So I get a huge deal with a potential customer and they want to order 100 items and easyship tells them that we don't offer shipping to their country and I lose the customer for life?!? Who decided this was a good message? Sounds like a great way to lose business! I emailed easyship to see what is going on and didn't hear back... (In their defense, I did email on a Saturday morning, but I still hadn't heard anything at 4pm Monday). So, I opened live chat and spoke with someone who tells me that "this is a limitation of their software" and the only option is to use a Shopify flat rate shipping for larger orders. Who considers 10+ items too large of an order? So in other words, I just wasted about 5hrs of my life trying to use their app only to have it require me to go back to the way it was without the app. Again, don't waste your time with easyship... The name itself is false advertising :(

classification.summary: Merchant invested 5+ hours and $800 in setup only to discover Easyship's calculated shipping fails on orders over 10 items with poor error messaging, unresponsive support, and no viable solution, forcing abandonment of the platform.

content.body: We've been working with Shipbob since 2018 and there are several reasons why Shipbob has been the perfect partner. First of all, speed to scale with us, pick-pack and ship with a business growing 1200% in a couple of weeks. Second of all, the locations across the US. We sells flammable goods that need to be shipped via ground, so Shipbob has been a great ally as they have 3pls all over the US facilitating 2-3 day delivery time for any customer in the US. Specially when weather challenges happen, being able to have different 3pls to ship from, allows you to ensure you seamless supply chain.Also their advanced software help us to quickly understand shipping costs and how to improve shipping times and costs by being closer to where your customer.

classification.summary: Long-term ShipBob customer (since 2018) praising their ability to scale rapidly, their multi-location network for fast ground shipping of flammable goods, and their software for optimizing shipping costs and delivery times.

content.body: Works great. Self-serve platforms are the standard for modern 3PLs and ShipBob is at the top. They've scaled seamlessly with our volumes, that have grown significantly over the last 3 years.

Overall Shipbob is amazing, the above comments are all growing pains and I am guessing will be sorted out over time. Shipbob can handle the easiest to the most complex shipping issues!! Try them you will not be disappointed.

content.body: We really enjoy working with the ShipBob dashboard on a daily basis. For our customer service team, it's a simple way to keep track of the stage of every package (from picking, to shipping, to delivery), and to provide these details to our customers when necessary. ShipBob has also made it super simple for us to be able to file claims with carriers when we've had issues with them, and we love their carriers estimated shipping time tool (especially during peak time season). Their dashboard is easy to understand and makes changes and edits to orders simple. We've also loved working with their team, both with our team lead as well as with every customer service rep we've spoken to on the phone. Through their dashboard and analytics, we've also been able to analyze our inventory and distribution of it to our different warehouses. Overall, would highly recommend.

content.body: We have been using ShipBob for more than 1 year now and we are absolutely happy with their services. The dashboard is easy to use, everything automated so you can do other work while they are taking care of our order's fulfillment, and moreover, their support is just amazing! They are very responsive and resolve the problems as soon as you request.Their pricing is decent and it is so easy to use. I am not a tech person at all, and if I can use it, everyone can use it :) I highly recommend ShipBob!

classification.summary: Satisfied merchant praising ShipBob's ease of use, automation, responsive support, and competitive pricing after 1+ year of service.

content.body: We've used ShipBob ever since we launched 3 years ago and we couldn't be happier with our partnership. Their analytics dashboard is fascinating and user-friendly to pull data, track shipments, and track inventory. Our products have shipped to over 40 countries and ShipBob has had no issue. ShipBob is not just a 3PL, they are a true partner. They also do a good job of seeing areas of improvement for my company. They’ve introduced me to 4–5 companies that they think could be beneficial in specific areas, such as marketing or online reviews. You can’t really put a price on people I never would’ve met if it weren’t for having a good partnership with ShipBob. ShipBob does a good job of treating every brand they work with as a brand of their own. They’re like a large portfolio overseeing the development of different brands, and you can tell that they’re people you can trust. They’re not just throwing my products in poly mailers and sending them out; they’re a much more in-depth company.

classification.summary: Long-term satisfied customer praising ShipBob's user-friendly analytics, reliable international fulfillment, partnership approach, and value-add services like business introductions.

content.body: This is super easy and the customer service has been fantastic. We've been a long time customer of Shipbob and despite growing very fast they've kept up extremely well and have been great to work with.

classification.summary: Long-time ShipBob customer praising their easy-to-use platform and responsive customer service during a period of rapid business growth.

content.body: Using ShipBob for almost two years now. Seamless Shopify integration. Orders go directly to the ShipBob dashboard and are marked 'fulfilled' in Shopify once they're ready. We will keep working with ShipBob. They keep improving to continuously get even better. Really recommend using ShipBob.

classification.summary: Long-time ShipBob customer from the Netherlands praising seamless Shopify integration, reliable fulfillment, and continuous product improvements over two years.

content.body: We have used Shipbob for the past 4 months after migrating from a more antiquated fulfillment organization. We evaluated 5 fulfillment organizations prior to choosing Shipbob. There are 3 major reasons we chose Shipbob. First, Shipbob gave us the ability to analyze how to distribute our product across multiple warehouses in different parts of the US. Currently, we use 2 locations and are debating with Shipbob about moving our products into a 3rd location. The primary advantage of multiple locations has been reduced shipping cost, however with the winter storms and COVID we have also been able to fulfill out of an alternative location when one of the locations has been offline. This process has been automated and seamless which has saved us from a major customer satisfaction issue. The second reason we chose Shipbob is that they are truly partnering with us. They have made recommendations (location, box size, etc..) to help us reduce costs. Their pricing transparency and reporting is excellent and has helped us close our monthly reporting days earlier than with our prior fulfillment vendor. Lastly, we chose Shipbob because of its ease of integration and Dashboard. Everything from setting it up to running your shipping, changing addresses, chasing down customer sat issues etc... is available to us in an easy to use, intuitive, user friendly dashboard. Ultimately, we are very pleased with our decision to partner with Shipbob.

classification.summary: Skinesa switched to ShipBob from a legacy fulfillment provider and praises their multi-warehouse distribution capabilities, cost optimization partnership, pricing transparency, and user-friendly dashboard integration.

content.body: This app is so easy to use. It does all the work in the background. Orders are automatically added from Shopify to ShipStation...NO double entry of orders needed! We have it connected to our USPS/UPS/FedEx accounts and for $9 a month, this is one of the best decisions you can make. ShipStation also creates a packing slip which we include with all the orders for a customer receipt.

classification.summary: Bennington Museum Store praises ShipStation for its ease of use, automatic order integration with Shopify, carrier connectivity, and excellent value at $9/month.

content.body: The integration works smoothly & whenever we've needed customer service, Shipbob has been speedy in responding.

classification.summary: Barefoot Scientist is satisfied with ShipBob's smooth integration and responsive customer service.

content.body: ShipBob is the 3rd fulfillment partner that we've migrated to in 4 years of growing our operations. Compared to what we've used in the past (plus about half a dozen options we only reviewed, but never used), they're really putting the users first. They're not just a logistics/freight company offering ecom fulfillment - they really give us the tools and options we need for our business. Whether it's inventory insights, custom packaging and marketing inserts, analytics and exports, or helping us optimize our pricing by figuring out which of their warehouses to use, it's really, really nice to have a fulfillment partner like ShipBob on our side.

classification.summary: Established merchant with 4 years of operations highly satisfied with ShipBob's user-centric approach, business tools, and strategic warehouse optimization compared to previous fulfillment partners.

content.body: ShipBob's app integrates very well with Shopify and allows our order flow to automatically (*there is a slight sync delay*) appear in ShipBob's order management software. From there, the orders are picked, packed and shipped. This automated integration is key to any small business that needs their 3PL to do the heavy lifting! ShipBob is a massive player in the 3PL space and you should definitely give them a look to see if they can satisfy your needs and the needs of your customer.

classification.summary: Megababe praises ShipBob's seamless Shopify integration and automated order management capabilities as essential for small business fulfillment operations.

content.body: As far as 3pls go, Shipbob is the best I've worked with! In my personal opinion, more than cheap shipping rates and automatic fulfillment, having insight into exactly what's happening at the warehouse level is my top priority. Shipbob makes this possible with their software-- absolutely unparalleled. It provides full transparency into each stage of the pick & pack process and when there are issues (impossible to avoid in logistics), our Merchant Success Manager is just an email, phone call, or text away as well as support which is pretty responsive to my requests. I'm physically 10 timezones removed from where we ship from so that's super helpful. Shipbob's claim/reimbursement policy for lost or damages parcels is super fair, whereas other 3PLs I've worked with can leave you with huge bills, missing stock, and no way to find out why or where your products are. I love shipbob!

classification.summary: Australian merchant highly satisfied with ShipBob's warehouse visibility, responsive support, and fair claims policy, positioning it as superior to other 3PLs they've used.

content.body: Late December I contact 3 warehouse manager software Ship Hero, SKU Vault, and Finale Inventory. I spent 15 hours meeting with the 3 companies and meeting with 2 3PL one worked with Ship Hero the other with Sku Vault. By far Ship Hero is the best option for Shopify merchants. It's almost like it was build for it. We had to leave the 3PL that we were using within weeks and setup a 4000sq. warehouse. Reasons for leaving:1. 3PL had 1.5%-3% order error in peak months. 2. picker and packer were not efficient.3. Inventory was never correct.4. Shipping Amazon orders took too long5. No API integration with Shopify Plus. The solution: Ship Hero1. Error margin 0.01%2. picker and packer positions cut in half3. inventory is accurate 99%4. Integration with Amazon5. Ship Hero and Shopify Plus connect in minutes. If it wasn't for Ship Hero and Yosef Haas we wouldn't have been able to setup a warehouse in weeks. They advised us in all purchases from Racks to Scanners.During the learning phase (3 weeks) we meet with Ship Hero almost everyday. Onboarding team is extremely knowledgable and responsive. They don't let you struggle through setup mode which is already hard as it is. I couldn't be any happier or more satisfied with their software. Thanks Yosef PS. If Ship Hero didn't exist I would never want to own a warehouse.

classification.summary: Merchant switched from a 3PL with poor accuracy and inefficiency to ShipHero for superior order accuracy, inventory management, Shopify Plus integration, and exceptional onboarding support.

content.body: If I could give more, they'll get more...to make up for these 1-star toxic reviews. Be reasonable people. The setup can take some time as with many TP services/plugins, but the team at Easyship are extremely friendly and will go out of their way to help you if you're nice and easy to deal with too. I'd recommend setting up a demo phone call, keep a good relationship (as you should with business), and you'll have a seamless experience. They offer some serious analytical tools for an extra, But the basic free package is good enough. Good courier pricing as well. Personally, I've dealt with much third-party software, and the two account managers, Michelle and Shivanjali were top-notch. 10 out of 10. Definitely ask for their help if you're based in the States or APAC. Cheers guys, keep up the good work. Look forward to any new features. -Cal

content.body: DO NOT BOTHER with this waste of time shipping middle man. Over 75% of all shipments made through them have had problems. Now I know that they are just a middle man acting as agents for courier companies... but therein lies the problem. when you have a problem with your shipment, and I GUARANTEE you will, the only way to contact them is to open a support ticket which takes two days to receive a reply which is usually incomplete or inaccurate. the latest incident was a package picked up on 12/15/20 with a 2 - 5 day delivery service... 21 days later this is still not delivered and all I get from the email reply to my service request is "this in normal...we will look into it".... after three previous replies asking for confirmation that I want assistance. meanwhile my customers are leaving negative reviews because of their service.Bottom line.... do not install this app or use this joke of a freight middle man. Yes their rates are good... but dissatisfied customers leaving negative reviews about your company do not make up for the pennies saved.

Fortunately I have only one insurance claim on the go as I wouldn't have the time or headspace to chase up several....It has been over two months of back and forth emails.As I said I put the odd cock up from UPS down to what I am not losing through instore pilferage. It would really help if the couriers photographed the door (with GPS coordinates) they are delivering to, instead of grabbing a 'signature' which doesn't really help much.

content.body: I would NEVER recommend this fulfillment company to anyone... especially a startup that is trying to build their reputation. Shipmonk has been the biggest headache for me. I am the founder of my company and have direct interaction with the Shipmonk team and software on a daily basis. They have lost my product (numerous times) and blame me for it every time. Never apologizing even once it is found. They literally LOSE my product and I have to pay for their team to look for it. They charge extreme amounts for a service that is hardly fulfilled. "Over promise, Under deliver" is how I would rate Shipmonk. They do a great job at selling you on their services and once you move all your inventory and really invest in them, they start treating you like you're worth nothing to them. I currently have orders backed up for 7 days when their expected shipping timeframe is 3 days or less. No apologies just excuses. "It's a busy time of year." they say. I never take the time to write reviews but I felt this was absolutely necessary. I hope you consider ALL OTHER options before partnering with Shipmonk. Please feel free to call me directly if you'd like a more personal review of this company. 954-707-0665

content.body: I would summarise the issues with this shipment provider, all based on our work with them for almost 10 months. 1) You will never see transparent pricing, they display only shipment and handling fees to you, the rest is being charged on the background. So your actual bill is never known, until u dig into this.2) Loosing packages all the time, in our case is $300 worth of parcel. When they do this here is the scenario:- If insured, claim will be reviewed for months, till now we still wasn't reimbursed for any;- If not insured, NEVER takes responsibility, saying "not our fault, but logistics provider, so we won't do anything". While this is them who is service provider for us, legally we ask them, they ask logistics company, bc if we message to lets say DHL, their response will be "u r not our client". The normal practice is when your shipment is not insured, at least shipping cost is supposed to be refunded as service you paid for wasn't delivered, but not with these guys;3) If they made a mistake on inventory side, they will ask you for $40/hour so some person in warehouse could fix it. So you should pay for mistakes they make;4) Customer support, unresponsive and incompetent. If certain cases are addressed in time, they still can be resolved, but not with these guys. What shipmonk support does is basically trying just to reply anything or blaming anyone else instead of addressing the issue, probably for their internal records. Connecting to the shipmonk will bring you only headache. I was really surprised how such a low quality service can be provided by a US company.

content.body: What fulfillment company locks you into a contract and charges you to get your goods back if you're not pleased with their service!? content.body: We have been using their international shipment for 3 months. Overall, the experience is positive. Our initial shipments were for our Indiegogo campaign. They worked with us to use batch shipment to save cost. After that, we are using their regular service for our daily orders. Their UI interface gives you a clear view of shipping cost and duty tax for each available shipping option. Although there are some glitches with the app, they will work with you to fix them quickly. They don't own warehouse, so, all the pick and pack work you will work directly with the 3rd party warehouse that they recommend. Although initially, we were worried about communication issues between 3 parties, it turns out to be a none-issue. We are very happy with the warehouse they recommended. Suggestion for improvement to them is the post-shipment support. You will be getting typical scripted answers many times. But that make you frustrated especially for orders that are stuck or missing. If Easyship wants to be differentiated from their competitors, establishing an effective communication channel with the couriers that they provide can be a game changer. Because that will allow them to provide more info to the clients when a shipment is running into issues during shipment. By the way, have a good relationship with your account manager is important. They have been very helpful in every occasion.

classification.summary: Merchant is satisfied with Easyship's international shipping and warehouse partner after 3 months of use, but suggests improving post-shipment support and courier communication to better differentiate from competitors.

content.body: I'm honestly amazed about how smooth and easy the experience of working with ShipMonk has been so far. They ship everything in a timely manner, most of the time only hours after the orders are placed. The logistics used are great, and orders are being delivered super fast. Their support team is great, from the first person I talked to when I was doing some quotes, to everyone in the onboarding team and happiness engineers. They are super helpful and polite! The onboarding experience is well made to ensure they only ship the right pending orders on Shopify (not all 3PL companies worry about that, and it's critical to avoid errors when switching from another company or another fulfillment model). Dashboard looks clean and very well built. Fulfillment is not an easy task, but they made it easy to understand and find what you are looking for very fast. The algorithm can help a lot with inventory forecasting. Honestly, I have only good things to say. I'm a customer for less than a month, but so far, they were perfect. I don't usually write long reviews, but they deserved this. Keep it up, and congrats on the high-quality job. You awesome people at ShipMonk really make a difference and a big positive impact on a business. Thank you so much!

classification.summary: Highly satisfied new ShipMonk customer praising fast shipping speed, excellent support team, smooth onboarding process, and intuitive dashboard after switching from another fulfillment provider.

A month to get set up, various bills over £200 no explanation. Truly terrible experience, STILL waiting!!!,,,,,,..............

content.body: UPDATE - 29 SEPT 2020 - so it turns out that Easyship use totally the wrong HS product codes for international shipments. A shipping app that can't cope with HS Codes??!! Really??? So, even if you have the correct HS Codes for your products in Shopify, Easyship say "due to a technical limitation, the HS code will not always pull through correctly, meaning a discrepancy between the information on Shopify and what is converted to Easyship". This means all your Commercial Invoices will be wrong. Surely getting this right is a comedy basic of any shipping app claiming to be a shipping app??! It's greatly taken the shine off what was a good experience until now...we only found this out because of a parcel getting stopped by customs with a question about a product's HS Code...not ideal!Unfortunately it also turns out there's no way to update your product listings in Easyship to fix this either...so you are knowingly giving customs the wrong info. Not great. In fact pretty poor. 2ND SEPT 2020We just installed Easyship to help us manage the UPS shipping process for our UK based store. We're really glad we did!! UPS can't integrate easily (understatement!) so we were having to manually enter every shipment into the UPS website (arrgh). Easyship solved this problem for us relatively easily and now works like a dream. Not only does Easyship make our shipping workflow/paperwork much easier and faster but it also solves our dynamic pricing at checkout so customers automatically see our special UPS shipping rates alongside our Royal Mail rates...awesome! Our only feedback would be that setting it up required quite a lot of research and there were some really helpful (although slightly out of date) videos on the Easyship YouTube channel - and we defo couldn't have done it without these. They steered us in the right direction and we were able to fill in most set-up gaps. What would be really useful is a step-by-step set up video guide for a Shopify Store if someone can make one so that all the set up steps are in one place. We linked to our UPS account and rates really easily and the initial glitch we had with creating labels/UPS tracking was resolved quickly after an online chat with Easyship support - they were super helpful and took time to understand the issue and help us get it fixed and followed up. The system is easily customisable and user friendly. All in all a really good experience - highly recommend. P.S. If we had one request it would be to work on integration with Royal Mail Click & Drop in the UK.

classification.summary: UK-based merchant initially satisfied with Easyship's UPS integration and dynamic pricing but discovered critical technical flaw where HS codes don't pull through correctly for international shipments, causing customs issues and wrong commercial invoices with no way to fix it.

content.body:
UPDATE 9/9/2020
DO NOT DO NOT DO NOT!!!! GO THRU SHIPSTATION!!!!
I decided to give them another chance, even though the packages would come back with overage charges (one yard sign, purchase price $13.50, shipping cost was 10.85, overage charges of $108.75!!!)  Customer service Maia, told me that she would take care of it.  We contacted UPS, and they said too bad.  But Shipstation took care of me...., that was for the original review.
So, I decided to protect myself by taking pics of EVERY SINGLE PACKAGE that leaves here, all the same packages.  And again, MORE overage charges.So I went ahead contact Shipstation, sent pictures and stated I was being overcharged again.  All of my packages are the same size, they always ship out daily, I spent over $400 in shippng yesterday ALONE.  And this is the customer service I get...I was hoping they would help....NOPE!  This was the response from Shantice D., after I told her I am ready to leave if they aren't going to protect me from all of these ridiculous charges  	Shantice D. (ShipStation)Sep 9, 2020, 10:35 AM CDTHi Julie,
Thanks for your reply. I do apologize for any inconvenience, however, this decision is ultimately up to the carrier based on the dimensions entered compared to what UPS says they actually are.
We appreciate you working with us. Take care and have a great day.
Best,Shantice D.User Success SpecialistCheck out our upcoming Support Webinars"
Needless to say...go to shippo or even contact the carriers directly.  The hidden charges are more expensive than going thru shipstation, in the long run.
This review should be a negative 5 star....they should have to give me stars!absolutely WORST customer service ever (see below for transcript)!  Go to shippo, go straight to the carriers, hell drive your orders out to your customers.  THIS IS NOT THE APP TO DO IT!!!
I ship alot of packages every day....so far this month 321 packages (not bad for a little old lady sitting at home).  And I was looking for a way to mainline my shipping process.  I came across this program and have had nothing but trouble.  But NOTHING beats this week!
They charged me $120 for ONE package that was shipped about an hour away.  Now this package weighed LESS THAN 2 lbs, and had dimensions of 25x19x1.  and when I questioned it, I was basically told "too bad, deal with it".  So I want an explanation.  They claim that I was being hit by all of these backcharges at once, but NO explanation on what the back charges were???
I questioned and questioned over and over again.  STILL no answer.  Here is the latest correspondence trying to find out why i was being charged so much?  Same size package shipped 45mins away (really wish I just drove it myself), charged $10 originally, and $17 back charge???? FOR WHAT????
Transcript:
someone must get back to me today about these hidden fees of over 120 I was charged.Chat startedSupport TeamThanks for chatting in, we're working on finding the next available agent for you!
Clara H. joined the chatClara H.Hi Julie, our team is still looking into this for you and Maia is in contact with you via emailAll updates related to your case will be sent to you there
I expect someone to explain these "hidden back charges to me"./  there is NO acceptable reason that I should pay 120 for a box that weighed LESS THAN 2 lbs and had dimension of 25x19x1Clara H.I'm happy to let Maia know that you're looking for an update, is there anything else that I can help you with here in chat?
i already received her "message" yesterday which gave ZERO explanation just yes these are the charges, deal with it.  one package cost me 28 dollars to ship...and this was being delivered 45 mins away!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!Clara H.I can see your responses to the ticket from last night, Maia will be sure to follow up with you
i ship a lot of packages every day...and i am seriously looking into other shipping programs unless someone explains these hidden charges, todaydo i pay extra because these are residential addresses?if so, why isnt' that on the front end, how much additional is it for resi?Clara H.I definitely understand where you're coming from, but I do not have access to those details
you have answers to those last 2 questionsClara H.I do not have access to those details and your case has been escalated to one of our team leads, Maia, who is working to resolve this for you
so you don't know if u charge for residential addresses?Clara H.I will let her know that you're looking for a response as soon as possible
you need a new job because that is a general question and everyone should know thatClara H.Is there anything else I can assist you with while we're here in chat? I'm happy to help how I can, but for updates on this case, please refer to your original ticket so that we can help you as efficiently as possible
i am going to be posting this message all over the internet so they can see what customer services DOESNT look likeClara H.One moment please.

content.body: We have been Ship Station customers for years, and apart from being somewhat buggy we have never had any real problems.We changed to a Shopify store a year ago, and I need to integrate Shopify with Ship Station.Now, I realize why there are all these complaints about their customer service.I have requested a call back from a tech support person on two occasions and it hasn't happened either time.  The first time they actually made an appointment to call, but never did.The second time they were supposed to email to set up an appointment. It has been a week and no email.The support we have received via Live Chat has been good, but should there be a serious problem with integration, I have no confidence that I could ever reach a live person to resolve it.When we started with Ship Station, we had an account manager who helped us set it up. Now, like every other tech company, they think its OK to skimp on live support to save money. Unfortunately for them, there are lots of other options available now, and we will be looking at alternatives.

content.body:
Lost 3 hours of my sleep time trying to generate a set of shipping labels and in frustration left a scathing review of the service in frustration.
What happened next changed my perspective about the Easyship team.The customer service & tech teams were super reactive in investigating the issue, resolving it and at the time communicating promptly with me(including phone calls) as regards what was causing the issue in the first place and what they were doing to fix it.
The fault in the first place was not entirely theirs..it was the Hermes API that was rejecting long SKU codes and hence the issue).Overall, few improvements yet to be introduced including the option to print labels in-store and better rates negotiation with the courier guys but if they work the way they are at the moment, they will win hearts and businesses in the long run. Highly reccomend these guys.

content.body: I originally wrote this review under the wrong app.  Wanted to put it in the correct place. Overall we have been relatively disappointed with this product. It is extremely powerful but is missing basic functionality that can make it or break it for your company depending on your business model. We operate with a lot of backorders, when stock comes in and is processed in for receipt, we need our oldest orders (customers who have been patiently waiting) to get their items first. The ship hero system randomly decides where to allocate it with no ryme or reason. It generally will skip tons of older orders and allocate it to newer orders; it will randomly give a couple to a bigger order and skip the rest, it just doesnt make any sense how it works. In many cases it will remove allocation from someone that has been waiting and give their products to other people. The system just simply needs to work where it allocates stock to the oldest orders first, if the user chooses to move an order up to a higher priority then they can do that themselves. If it wasn't for this constant struggle we are having with this allocation fiasco we would give it five stars.

classification.summary: HCC Tactical is frustrated with ShipHero's allocation logic for backorders, which randomly assigns stock to newer orders instead of respecting FIFO (oldest orders first) and manual priority settings, preventing a 5-star rating despite the platform's power.

content.body:
Has the basis of being great but so many glitches and the amount of additional charges that come in each week in addition to the shipping charges is unreasonable. I regularly had issues with no shipping options being available for a lot of stock, so I would consistently have to book a courier outside the platform, defeating the purpose of the app.
One big issue was the inability to ship an order that has 2 boxes, only option is to ship them separately at double the cost- this is something they said they were working on months ago that had a 2 week deadline but it is still not an active feature.

classification.summary: Easyship merchant in Australia is actively switching to external couriers due to persistent platform glitches, unexpected recurring charges, and inability to efficiently ship multi-box orders despite months of promised fixes.

content.body: Where do I start! the idea and roadmap they have are great but what they say they offer doesn't work. We are UK based and find Fundamental things like the invoices are wrong as they show the price and the tax added together as the order total so the customer thinks they are paying more than they are. Shipstation told me this was fixed in version 3 but it isn't. The support they offer is poor. I have been trying to get set up for 5 weeks and the person I am speaking with who I have to book in a 30min appointment with once a week doesn't know the system and has to create support tickets for every issue I have. they have issues with exporting data, booking in certain labels like royal mail jersey or DPD Ireland. The automation rules they have are hit and miss and it seems to take time to make amendments. you have to reload the page numerous times for things to take effect.I would be more than happy to change my rating if these things are addressed. I have tried with support but when you get an email in reply to a phone call asking if I still have a problem to get in touch. I wouldn't have contacted them in the first place if I didn't have a problem (i would just like some help). I'm trying to give them my money and they aren't able to help me to do it. for anyone thinking about moving to ship station its not a 1 click setup and there is an awful lot of work to do to get things working as you need and if you're like me you will find some basic features are missing.

content.body: Update as at 26 April 2020:Gesselle and her team have been a great help and a real delight in assisting me on tracking down my DHL E-commerce parcel that would have otherwise been lost and I'm extremely grateful for that. content.body: We've been using Easyship since we first launched. In the years that have followed, Easyship has proven to be a super simple and functional addition to our store, which lets us tell people that we ship anywhere that receives mail. The only downside with Easyship is that customer service can be slow at times and seems to be email only, which can add a day or two to responses.

If you want a full system for running your e-commerce business, you found it.

We do subscription boxes.

content.body:
Horrible shipping solution. Riddled with bugs, it seems like there is a new problem every day. Customer service is unresponsive, or if they do respond they are unable/unwilling to help. Save yourself a giant headache and find a better company to work with.
In response to EasyShip's reply to our comment. We brought up an issue with Amazon integration multiple times, and each time were told that they would reach out when their was a resolution. Every week for over a month I received an automatic response saying the ticket would be closed due to lack of my response. I would bump the ticket and receive a response of "thank you for the reminder, we are waiting to hear back from our team". Nothing ever got taken care of.
EasyShip has reached out to me multiple times to get this review removed, but has made no effort to show the issues I brought up were actually resolved.

content.body: The app is easy to use and pretty straightforward. I like how you can email tracking information to customers directly from the shipping information page. content.body: We started using this app when we moved from a 3PL to our own warehouse. It has helped us ship all the orders in time and without making mistakes.The team has been excellent and has guided us throughout the process.Shiphero is one the best inventory and shipping software I've used so far.

classification.summary: Satisfye is praising Easyship for enabling their global business growth and appreciates the responsive, supportive team.

content.body:
Heed these warnings... if you like to stay in business.
We have been using Shipmonk for over 2 years. Their customer service is ok and so is their application but they will kill your startup with their ridiculous minimums and storage fees. To make it worse they you cannot trust what shipping they quote you, they will come back and charge 2 to 3X the original price quoted on the order. The latest example being charging $55 when the quote was $26. If I re-do the same order I am still shown $26. Its essentially fraud.
Also their minimum fees is a ridiculous concept. This is what I got from their support "when storage exceeds $250.00 your minimum increases to the storage cost". They already charge a premium on storage and they you end up paying 2X that because invariably your storage fees will cross usd 250 (unless you are shipping really small items with limited number of SKUs) and then that would become your new minimum, essentially doubling your storage fees. While they claim this crucial clause was always there on their website (we don't remember reading it) their sales agent never bothered to mention it during the initial meeting we had and it is not in our contract.
Also it is nearly impossible to know what you are paying for when it comes to storage "their system" essentially decides and there is very little visibility as to why you are paying of X number of pallets or Y number of bins. Also you would be paying for a whole pallet (X2 because of the minimums) even if you have 1 unit left on it and to make it worse you don't know so you can't do anything about it.
It is very hard to change a warehouse once you are stuck with one so do your research before you make the mistake we did.
Find someone else unless you love throwing away money. Companies like Shipmonk should go out of business so companies that do care about their customers can do well.
Echo in the line from a different review because we had to deal with the exact issue, thousands of dollars worth of lost inventory after they signed for it:"Some examples: They lose inventory upon receiving, because they don't check what they sign for when product is delivered to them vs. what they actually receive and enter into the system. So when shipments show as delivered but the inventory is missing from the warehouse, their excuse is that they receive too many shipments to possibly try to check what was actually received. They have literally, on multiple occasions, lost thousands of dollars of inventory, and even when escalated to the CEO, refuse to accept ownership of this or do anything about it. And because they signed the delivery slip, you can no longer claim for a lost package from the shipper."
One more thing.. do not trust the review posted on capterra .com. Capterra is a paid service and they will only post reviews they like, this review never showed up there even though it was posted there over 3 months ago, didn't even get an acknowledgement.

classification.summary: Long-time ShipMonk customer is actively switching due to hidden fees, storage minimums that double charges, opaque billing, and unreliable shipping quotes that exceed estimates by 2-3X, describing the practice as fraudulent.

content.body: I have been sourcing for a good complete package shipping partner for years that is easy to use and provide local and international courier services.Easyship provide a really good platform for my customers to use with ease, both locally and internationally. My customers can have options to choose which courier and cost of shipping they like to use. We the business owners can easily sync the orders with Easyship with a few clicks and the courier services is deal done in a few minutes. Most importantly, the service support I get from the local team is very good. Whenever I have an issue, they promptly solved it quickly so We can move on with our business.  Its been a good business partner.

classification.summary: Singapore-based merchant praising Easyship for its easy-to-use platform, local and international courier options, seamless order syncing, and responsive local customer support.

content.body:
Stop already with this nonsense. You overcharged. You want to blame me. You keep doing it even a year later. Stop with the madness. I'm not interested in doing business with you. I have no interest in hearing more from you. Just stop.
No there was no confusion as EasyShip states in the reply from over a year ago.  I've been shipping for years - I didn't input "mistake" dimensions. It's not difficult. Funny enough, I use Pirate Ship and haven't had any issues or mistakes. PirateShip is proactive, easy to use, great rates, no issues. The way shipping should be.
Just did a cost estimate shipping a box weighing 4 lbs from Mpls, MN to Madison WI. I have been paying about $6.55 for the last 12 months. The cost estimate I got from Easyship was $26.29. Looks like I'd be out of business quickly using this app. So sick of apps advertising "the best pricing" when in fact the pricing is awful. Sellers are not idiots.

content.body:
After over a year of searching and researching to find a robust, 2.0, inventory software management tool with 3PL features I had a friend recommend Shiphero. After a brief intro call I was intrigued, after the full demo I was blown away. Now, 1 month into our full integration and the software does what it says and more.
There is a level of thoughtfulness and organization that has gone into building Shiphero for the modern e-commerce business. From the sell ahead feature to the automation of shipping methods to every single field and feature. Shiphero allows us to create the rules that will help us continue to run an efficient warehouse without hindering our Sales and Marketing efforts.
Like any robust software there are nuances and tricks one must learn, but with patience and time even these nuances were well thought out and exist for a reason.
I also wanted to acknowledge the amazing support staff. Our account rep Matt has gone above and beyond on multiple occasions to help us as we learn the system. The customer service is hands down the most impressive I have ever experienced.
I am very happy and I have already recommended Shiphero to several of my friends in e-commerce.
5 stars is well deserved.

content.body: UPDATED REVIEW: HIDDEN FEES! BEWARE! The app has started charging shippers "residential fees" which are never disclosed beforehand. Easy ship obviously realized their mistake and started adding a "residential fee" option before checkout to make it easier, but then they discontinued this feature and I am being charged these hidden fees again! This is absolutely ridiculous.

classification.summary: Merchant is actively frustrated with Easyship's undisclosed residential fees and lack of transparency, indicating intent to switch providers due to hidden charges and poor fee management.

content.body:
I agree with the feedback left by DreamCastle. It takes three months for the billing from FedEx to be updated to Easyship billing. I am charged when creating labels one amount based upon the actual weight and box dimensions when shipping to the customers. Three months later a bill arrives with numerous adjustments to the shipments and in most cases, and to no surprised, the actual and (true) weights and box dimensions increased the chargeable weight by FedEx. It's no wonder this is a free app and as for me, it doesn't work for our business.
Also, be aware you will be charged by FedEx for Residential Area Surcharge. This amount can be anywhere from $4.40 to just under $10.00. It's depends on how far the delivery is out of the way for the driver. This comes out of your pocket unless you pass this cost onto the customers. Then again, this increase most likely will have your customers shopping elsewhere online.
Be aware, Easyship will nickle and dime you to death. I'm done and will now use USPS and UPS offered by Shopify. It's cheaper.

classification.summary: Merchant is actively switching away from Easyship due to hidden FedEx billing adjustments that arrive months late, unexpected residential surcharges, and overall cost structure, now using USPS and UPS via Shopify instead.

content.body:
Having used Shipstation, Stitchlabs and several others before ShipHero, I cannot recommend ShipHero more highly.
ShipHero is a complete system for running your ecommerce operation. It is powerful, the system is fast and it's reliable and accurate.
There are a ton of features in ShipHero that we did not expect to find, it's a pretty comprehensive solution.
If you're shipping more than 200 orders a day, you won't find better software to run your Shopify business than ShipHero.
